reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Procurement / Purchase Order Submission
Technical HPC Action Required (based on visible text): Unknown (cannot determine from message alone)
Finance/Procurement Action Required: Yes (PO processing workflow)
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The sender has submitted Purchase Order MA2-4362647 and asked for confirmation of receipt. The message also provides EORI information (if needed) and Goods-In opening hours. Based on the visible ticket text alone, the safe and correct handling is to:
1) confirm receipt,
2) review the attached PO to determine what action is required (e.g., invoicing/procurement processing and whether any HPC-specific actions such as crediting resource hours apply),
3) route the PO to the appropriate Finance/Procurement workflow,
4) request any missing details required to proceed.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Verify attachment availability (internal)
   - Confirm a PO document is attached and readable in the ticketing system.
   - Confirm the PO number MA2-4362647 appears on the document.

2) Determine required processing path (internal)
   - From the PO document, identify:
     a) supplier / contact details (if not already clear),
     b) what is being purchased (e.g., equipment/services vs. HPC resource/usage credit),
     c) quantity/amount (if applicable),
     d) any project/account/cost-centre information required for processing.
   - If the PO indicates an HPC-specific deliverable (e.g., purchase of compute/resource hours), escalate/route to the HPC finance workflow and request confirmation of the target project/account and quantity if not explicit.
   - If the PO is general procurement, route to the standard Procurement/Finance mailbox.

3) Forward to Finance/Procurement (internal)
   - Forward the PO (and ticket context) to the appropriate Finance/Procurement contact point.
   - Include PO number in subject and internal note for traceability.

4) Respond to sender with confirmation of receipt (external)
   - Send a brief confirmation that the PO was received and is being processed.
   - Do NOT claim any internal processing step has completed unless it has been verified (e.g., do not claim hours were credited, accounts updated, invoices raised, etc.).

5) Ticket update and closure (internal)
   - Resolve the ticket once confirmation of receipt has been sent and the PO has been routed appropriately.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
Ask only if the PO/attachment does not clearly specify these items:
- Which project/account/cost centre should this be associated with?
- What exactly is being purchased (e.g., HPC resource/compute time vs. goods/services)?
- If compute/resource is being purchased: how many hours/units should be applied?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY (CONFIRMATION OF RECEIPT)

Subject: Re: Purchase Order MA2-4362647 – Confirmation of Receipt

Dear Stephen,

Thank you for your email.

We confirm receipt of Purchase Order MA2-4362647. We will review the attached PO and have forwarded it to the appropriate team for processing. If any further information is required, we will contact you.

Kind regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not state or imply that any resource/account changes have been made unless explicitly confirmed by the operator performing the action.
- Do not guess quantities, project codes, or required technical steps from the email alone when attachments are not visible to the assistant.
- EORI and Goods-In details do not require action unless Finance/Procurement requests them as part of the processing workflow.
response:

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Confirm receipt of the PO MA2-4362647.
2. Process the PO as per standard procedures.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Stephen,

Thank you for your email. We have received your purchase order MA2-4362647 and will process it accordingly.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the PO is processed following the standard procedures.
- Confirm receipt promptly to maintain good communication with the requester.

user_input:
Hello,
I am a user of CSD3 (ejs237, dp012) and recently noticed that I can no longer access my files at
/rds/user/ejs237/rds-dirac-dp012/ejs237/.
I was informed that the directory has been moved to
/rds/project/dirac_vol2/rds-dirac-dp012, but this location does not appear to be valid or accessible in my case.
Could you please let me know where my previous data have been moved?
Thank you very much for your help.
Best regards,
Eun-jin Shin
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Path & Symlink / RDS
Technical Action Required: Yes (diagnose path/symlink + confirm correct new location)
Escalation Required: Possibly (if storage path mapping needs staff-side confirmation)
Urgency: Medium (data access blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User can no longer access files under /rds/user/ejs237/rds-dirac-dp012/ejs237/ and has been told the directory moved to /rds/project/dirac_vol2/rds-dirac-dp012, but that new location is not valid/accessible for them. On CSD3, /rds is Lustre-based storage and user-visible paths under /rds/user/ejs237/ are often exposed via convenience symlinks under ~/rds. Therefore, the most likely cause is that the user is following an outdated or broken path/symlink after a storage reorganisation. Next steps are to (1) inspect the relevant symlinks, (2) identify the correct new target path, and (3) update the user’s link(s) accordingly.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm what the user is actually accessing
   Ask the user to run:
     hostname
     ls -ld /rds/user/ejs237
     ls -l ~/rds
   Purpose: confirm they are on CSD3 and see whether ~/rds contains a symlink for the relevant project area.

2) Identify whether the path is a symlink and where it points
   Ask the user to run (adjust name to the relevant project directory label they used before):
     ls -l ~/rds
     readlink -f ~/rds/rds-dirac-dp012
     ls -ld ~/rds/rds-dirac-dp012
   Expected signal:
     - If readlink fails or points to a non-existent location, the link is broken/outdated.
     - If it points somewhere valid but permissions deny, it may be a membership/ACL issue.

3) Determine the correct new location (helpdesk-side confirmation)
   Because the user reports /rds/project/dirac_vol2/rds-dirac-dp012 “does not appear valid”, do NOT assume the target.
   Use internal knowledge/tools (or storage team confirmation) to identify the correct canonical path for this project’s data on the current filesystem layout.

4) Update the user-facing link/path (only once the correct target is confirmed)
   If the correct target path has been confirmed, instruct:
     cd ~/rds
     rm rds-dirac-dp012
     ln -s <CONFIRMED_TARGET_PATH> rds-dirac-dp012
     ls -ld rds-dirac-dp012
   Notes:
     - Use rm only for the symlink in ~/rds, not for real directories.
     - If rds-dirac-dp012 is not in ~/rds but the user relies on a deep /rds/user path, provide the correct canonical path they should use going forward.

5) Validate access
   Ask the user to run:
     ls -lah ~/rds/rds-dirac-dp012
     ls -lah <CONFIRMED_TARGET_PATH>
   Confirm they can see expected directories/files.

IF THIS FAILS (LIKELY BRANCHES)

A) Permission denied at the new target
   - Confirm the user’s project membership and directory ACLs/groups.
   - Ask for:
       id
       groups
       ls -ld <CONFIRMED_TARGET_PATH>
   - Escalate to storage admins if ACL/group membership needs adjustment.

B) Target path truly does not exist
   - The “dirac_vol2” path the user was given may be incorrect or incomplete.
   - Re-check the correct storage mapping for that project (staff-side), then provide the correct path and update symlink accordingly.

C) User is using the wrong environment/path for I/O
   - Remind them that active data should live on /rds (not /home) and that /rds/user areas may be presented via ~/rds symlinks for convenience.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- What exact error do you see when accessing the old and new paths? (e.g., "No such file or directory" vs "Permission denied")
- What is the exact project directory name you used previously under /rds/user/ejs237/ ?
- Please paste output of:
    ls -l ~/rds
    readlink -f ~/rds/rds-dirac-dp012

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY (USER-FACING)

Subject: Re: Missing Files After RDS Directory Change?

Hello Eun-jin,

Thanks for getting in touch. On CSD3, many project directories under /rds/user/ejs237/ are typically accessed via convenience symlinks under ~/rds.

Could you please run the following and paste the output?

  ls -l ~/rds
  readlink -f ~/rds/rds-dirac-dp012

This will let us confirm whether the path you’re using is a broken/outdated symlink following the directory move. Once we confirm the correct new location for your project data, we can point your ~/rds link to the right place.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not guess the new canonical target path; confirm it before instructing link changes.
- Avoid destructive commands on /rds; only remove symlinks in ~/rds when you are certain they are symlinks.
- Do not claim files were moved or recovered unless you have explicit evidence of the correct new location and access has been validated.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Path Validation
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is unable to access their data at the specified path. The retrieved documents suggest that data migration and path changes are common issues. The path /rds/project/dirac_vol2/rds-dirac-dp012 is mentioned in a similar context, but it is not confirmed to be valid for this user's specific case [2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the current location of the user's data by checking the migration records or contacting the relevant team member.
2. Confirm the correct path for the user's data and provide it to the user.
3. If the path is confirmed to be /rds/project/dirac_vol2/rds-dirac-dp012, ensure the user has the necessary permissions to access it.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide any additional details about the migration or any communications you received regarding the new path?
- Have you tried accessing the data from a different login node or system?
- What specific error message do you receive when trying to access the path?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Eun-jin,

Thank you for your message. We are currently verifying the correct path for your data. In the meantime, could you please provide any additional details about the migration or any communications you received regarding the new path? This will help us assist you more effectively.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not provide a path without verifying its validity and the user's access permissions.
- Ensure that any path provided is consistent with the documented migration procedures and records.

Name: Matthew Roberts

My research group purchased 20 Tb storage on the RDS system at the start of the year under the PI gio10. However, I am struggling to locate this directory.
Currently, the working data that I am post processing is within the dp275 project (which is ending imminently) so I need to move the data to the new store in the next week or so.

Many thanks and please let me know if you need any further information.
Matt

On Sat, Jun 29, 2019 at 7:05 AM Stuart Rankin <[support@hpc.cam.ac.uk](mailto:support@hpc.cam.ac.uk)> wrote:
> —-—-—-—
> Reply above this line.
> Stuart Rankin commented:
> Hi Joe,
> I probably could have been clearer and a bit more helpful yesterday, sorry.
> When you were on Darwin you had a directory on lustre storage that was available under ~/scratch (which was a symbolic link, or shortcut, to the actual lustre fileystem directory). When we migrated users to CSD3 in late 2017, this data was copied to a new lustre filesystem, which since these now form part of the Research Data Store (RDS) service are available under ~/rds/hpc-work instead.
> I have taken a look at your ~/rds/hpc-work and your original scratch directory (the latter still exists but is not accessible to users) and superficially at least they look the same. If you think that there is something missing however, please give me the details and we can search for it in the original directory, however everything that was there should have made it to ~/rds/hpc-work.
> Chris also had additional storage, and this has been migrated to a separate RDS project directory. You should probably also look therefore in
> ~/rds/rds-cj107-heliconius
> I hope this makes sense -
> Best regards,
> Stuart

[TICKET_SUMMARY] dc-hand2 files on csd3

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hi guys,
I’m having some trouble migrating my data to CSD3. The issue is that the directory structure I see in
~/rds
And
/rds
Does not seem to contain any directory for me. For example, I’ve been looking for 
/rds/user/<username>/hpc-work  
As stated here: [https://www.csd3.cam.ac.uk/using-clusters/filesystems](https://www.csd3.cam.ac.uk/using-clusters/filesystems)
 
But the directory /rds/user/dc-hand2 contains only a folder called rds-dirac-005 which in turn contains the data of 4 of my Leicester colleagues. This hpc-work directory appears to be conspicuously absent.
 
Any help appreciated,
Tom

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Support with RDS group directory on CSD3

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Name: Matthew Roberts
Crsid: mjor2
My research group purchased 20 Tb storage on the RDS system at the start of the year under the PI gio10. I have been told the project is labelled as damtp-astro and is located in the “rds/rds-damtp-astro-xg257ahCixg and is visible from CSD3 login nodes”. However, I am struggling to locate this directory.
Currently, the working data that I am post processing is within the dp275 project (which is ending imminently) so I need to move the data to the new store in the next week or so.
Therefore, I have the following questions:
1.  Could you please provide guidance as to how to access this storage location. Currently I am unable to find it either in the rds directory or under the “quota” command
2.  Further, once I have access how can I easily access the directory from the login-web interface system ?
Many thanks and please let me know if you need any further information.
Matt

user_input:
Dear Storage Services team,
We would like to renew our licences for both the RDS and RCS  (account 80/81) for another year.
On top of this is it possible to change the ownership of these licences to Maria (Cc’ed) who is our new head of bioinformatics?
Many thanks
Brian
**
Dr. Brian Lam
Assistant Research Professor
Institute of Metabolic Science-Metabolic Research Laboratories
Medical Research Council Metabolic Diseases Unit
University of Cambridge
Institute of Metabolic Science
Level 4, Box 289, Addenbrooke's Hospital
Cambridge CB2 0QQ
United Kingdom
Phone: +44 (0)1223 768628
Email: yhbl2@cam.ac.uk<[yhbl2@cam.ac.uk](mailto:yhbl2@cam.ac.uk)>
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Licence Management
Technical Action Required: No
Portal Action Required: Yes (Self Service)
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requests renewal of RDS and RCS licences and transfer of licence ownership. These actions are handled via the Self Service Storage Portal.

ACTION
Direct user to:
https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/

Do not process renewal or ownership changes manually via ticket.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Brian,

These actions can be taken within the relevant storage licences on the Self Service Storage Portal:
https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/

Please let us know if you encounter any difficulties.

Best regards,  
HPC Support
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Licence Management
Technical Action Required: No
Portal Action Required: Yes (Self Service)
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
Licence renewal and ownership transfer are self-service actions in the Storage Portal, so manual processing via ticket is not required. [1][2]

ACTION
Direct the user to: https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/ [2]
Do not process renewal or ownership changes manually via ticket. [1]

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Brian,

These actions can be completed in the Self Service Storage Portal:
https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/ [2]

Please get back in touch if any portal step fails. [1]

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not perform ownership/licence changes manually when the documented self-service path applies. [1][2]

Dear officer,
I have some issues with login via this page. My colleague has added me as the co-leader in this project.
However, when I click on the " the “Sign in with Keycloak”" it didn't transfer me to the University of Liverpool credentials page. I tried to login anyway with my liverpool account but it doesn't work.
Could you kindly help me how should I login to this portal? Thank you very much for your time and help. Have a nice day.
Best regards,
Yingjie Wang

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / Portal Login
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (access blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is attempting to log in via “Sign in with Keycloak” and expects to authenticate using University of Liverpool credentials. The correct login method for external users is via the “MyAccessID” option beneath the login boxes.

ACTION
Instruct user to:
- Click the “MyAccessID” button under the login fields.
- Authenticate using their institutional credentials.
- Follow official documentation if required.

Do not:
- Reset credentials.
- Modify account settings.
- Escalate unless MyAccessID authentication fails.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Yingjie,

You need to click the “MyAccessID” button underneath the login boxes to log in with your university credentials.

Please see this guidance:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html#users-accessing-open-on-demand-via-the-portal

Best regards,
HPC Support
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Access / Login Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with logging in via the Keycloak sign-in page. They have been invited to a project but are unable to proceed with the login process. Similar issues have been reported in previous tickets [1][2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the user's invitation link and ensure it is still valid.
2. Check if the user has received any one-time code or additional instructions for login.
3. Confirm if the user has an existing account or needs to create a new one.
4. Provide the user with detailed steps for logging in, including any necessary account creation steps.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Have you received any one-time code or additional instructions for login?
- Do you have an existing account or are you attempting to create a new one?
- What specific error message are you seeing when attempting to log in?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Yingjie,

Thank you for reaching out. It appears you are experiencing issues with logging in via the Keycloak sign-in page. To assist you further, could you please provide the following information:

1. Have you received any one-time code or additional instructions for login?
2. Do you have an existing account or are you attempting to create a new one?
3. What specific error message are you seeing when attempting to log in?

Please let us know, and we will do our best to resolve this issue promptly.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user's invitation link is valid and has not expired.
- Provide clear and detailed instructions for logging in, including any necessary account creation steps.
- Do not share sensitive information such as one-time codes or passwords.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-101077::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-100575::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-98289::chunk::0000
[4] : HPCSSUP-100956::chunk::0000
[5] : HPCSSUP-88257::chunk::0000

user_input:
Dear HPC support team,
I was wondering if there is a way to copy paste text between local machine and the SRCP virtual desktop in browser? I've tried using the clipboard icon on the TurboVNC side toolbar, which is supposed to work as a medium for this, but it doesn't seem to sync properly (only picks up text from my local machine, cannot be pasted into the virtual desktop).
Is there an alternative method for transferring text?
Alternatively, is there a way to log into SRCP via ssh in the terminal? That would make text transfers and edits much easier.
Best,
Shilin
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SRCP / Secure Desktop / Data Transfer
Technical Action Required: No (policy clarification only)
Security Impact: Yes (clipboard isolation is intentional)
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is attempting to copy/paste text between their local machine and the SRCP virtual desktop (TurboVNC) and reports that clipboard sync does not function bidirectionally. They also ask whether SSH access to SRCP is possible as an alternative.

Clipboard isolation is enforced by design under SRCP security policy. Direct copy/paste between local and SRCP environments is not permitted. SSH access is not offered as a workaround.

ACTION
Inform user that:
- Copy/paste from outside SRCP into the platform is not permitted due to security policy.
- Data/text transfer must occur via approved secure channels.
- Provide documentation link for Secure Windows Desktop (SWD) guidance.

Do not:
- Suggest policy bypass.
- Recommend enabling clipboard sharing.
- Offer SSH access as an alternative method.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Shilin,

The copy-paste function from outside of the SRCP is not possible, as this would violate SRCP security policies.  

You can transfer data or files into the platform using the approved secure channels described here:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/srcp/swd/index.html

Best regards,  
HPC Support
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SRCP / Access Methods
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The SRCP platform is designed to prevent copying and pasting between the local machine and the virtual desktop for security reasons [1][2][3]. SSH access is not supported as an alternative to the browser-based virtual desktop [4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
- Inform the user that copying and pasting between the local machine and the SRCP virtual desktop is not supported by design [1][2][3].
- Inform the user that SSH access to the SRCP platform is not available [4].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Shilin,

Thank you for your inquiry.

The SRCP platform is designed to prevent copying and pasting between your local machine and the virtual desktop for security reasons [1][2][3]. This feature is intended to ensure data security and integrity.

Regarding SSH access, it is not supported as an alternative to the browser-based virtual desktop [4].

If you have any further questions or need additional assistance, please let us know.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not provide workarounds that bypass the intended security measures of the SRCP platform [1][2][3].
- Do not suggest or enable SSH access to the SRCP platform [4].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-56271::chunk::0001
[2] : HPCSSUP-19289::chunk::0001
[3] : HPCSSUP-79592::chunk::0001
[4] : HPCSSUP-80957::chunk::0000

[TICKET_SUMMARY] SRCP terminal

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Nick,
Not being able to copy and paste is an intended feature of the SRCP, so users cannot copy data into/out of the platform. If you launch a shell inside remote desktop then you should still be able to copy/paste within the environment. I believe this has always been the case. To scroll, try using shift with page up/down.
Best regards,
Jonathan

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0002 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Nick,
If users were able to copy/paste between their computer and SRCP, then this was not intended. Might it be that a particular OS and browser combination was able to work? We have tested with a number of combinations to ensure it does not work. We haven’t pushed out any changes to platforms for a while now. Were you curious or has this caused you an issue now?
Best regards,
Jonathan

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0003 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Let me know if you want to discuss this further, especially if you are facing an issue.
Best regards,
Jonathan

</MESSAGE>

Are you talking about pasting content from one part of the desktop to another, or from an application on your mac into the desktop (or vice versa)?
Also please confirm that you have tried disconnecting and reconnecting.
If that doesn't work then please try killing the desktop and restarting it.
I'm afraid that if we can't reproduce the issue this sort of generic advice is the best that we are going to be able to come up with.

</MESSAGE>

[TICKET_SUMMARY] SRCP EPI platform - pasting small number of characters into remote desktop

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=OTHER>
Hello Tom,
Thank you for submitting a ticket, I’ve passed the ticket over to Jonathan. Expect a response as soon as possible.
Best regards,
Elisabeth Reeve 
HPC Support

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0002 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Tom,
I cannot think of a way that this could be implemented, but you could have something on the client end that reads a text file and produces key strokes for each character in order the input data. Not the most elegant, or possibly not user friendly at all, but just an idea. I usually will tell people that the platform has a air lock between the client and server, so there is no way to get data between the two by design, but I can see your point that this becomes annoying to work with. I might suggest that development/testing happens outside the platform and then brought in.
Best regards,
Jonathan

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0003 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Hi Jonathan,
Thanks for giving it some thought. I understand about the design to only permit data flow via the airlock and wouldn't have opened the ticket had I not seen that some other TRE systems offer this. One example is Genomics England:
[https://re-docs.genomicsengland.co.uk/import/#copy-paste](https://re-docs.genomicsengland.co.uk/import/#copy-paste)
This system is described as a Linux Virtual Desktop Interface (VDI) hosted by AWS and a High Performance Compute (HPC) cluster:
[https://re-docs.genomicsengland.co.uk/re/#technical-information](https://re-docs.genomicsengland.co.uk/re/#technical-information)
I guess the architecture is different to the SRCP?
Cut and paste is also available in the NHS SDE:
[https://digital.nhs.uk/services/secure-data-environment-service/log-in/user-guides/using-databricks-in-sde/copying-and-pasting-into-sde#:~:text=Press%20Ctrl%20%2B%20V%20(Cmd%20%2B,The%20dialog%20box%20will%20disappear](https://digital.nhs.uk/services/secure-data-environment-service/log-in/user-guides/using-databricks-in-sde/copying-and-pasting-into-sde#:~:text=Press%20Ctrl%20%2B%20V%20(Cmd%20%2B,The%20dialog%20box%20will%20disappear).
Again, this is clearly different to the SRCP as it has a Windows desktop.
In terms of a priority, this is one I wanted to get on the backlog for consideration and is something for the longer term. Only a couple of users have asked for it so far.
Best wishes
Tom

</MESSAGE>

[TICKET_SUMMARY] SRCP EPI platform - pasting small number of characters into remote desktop

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=OTHER>
Hello Tom,
Thank you for submitting a ticket, I’ve passed the ticket over to Jonathan. Expect a response as soon as possible.
Best regards,
Elisabeth Reeve 
HPC Support

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0002 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Tom,
I cannot think of a way that this could be implemented, but you could have something on the client end that reads a text file and produces key strokes for each character in order the input data. Not the most elegant, or possibly not user friendly at all, but just an idea. I usually will tell people that the platform has a air lock between the client and server, so there is no way to get data between the two by design, but I can see your point that this becomes annoying to work with. I might suggest that development/testing happens outside the platform and then brought in.
Best regards,
Jonathan

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0003 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Hi Jonathan,
Thanks for giving it some thought. I understand about the design to only permit data flow via the airlock and wouldn't have opened the ticket had I not seen that some other TRE systems offer this. One example is Genomics England:
[https://re-docs.genomicsengland.co.uk/import/#copy-paste](https://re-docs.genomicsengland.co.uk/import/#copy-paste)
This system is described as a Linux Virtual Desktop Interface (VDI) hosted by AWS and a High Performance Compute (HPC) cluster:
[https://re-docs.genomicsengland.co.uk/re/#technical-information](https://re-docs.genomicsengland.co.uk/re/#technical-information)
I guess the architecture is different to the SRCP?
Cut and paste is also available in the NHS SDE:
[https://digital.nhs.uk/services/secure-data-environment-service/log-in/user-guides/using-databricks-in-sde/copying-and-pasting-into-sde#:~:text=Press%20Ctrl%20%2B%20V%20(Cmd%20%2B,The%20dialog%20box%20will%20disappear](https://digital.nhs.uk/services/secure-data-environment-service/log-in/user-guides/using-databricks-in-sde/copying-and-pasting-into-sde#:~:text=Press%20Ctrl%20%2B%20V%20(Cmd%20%2B,The%20dialog%20box%20will%20disappear).
Again, this is clearly different to the SRCP as it has a Windows desktop.
In terms of a priority, this is one I wanted to get on the backlog for consideration and is something for the longer term. Only a couple of users have asked for it so far.
Best wishes
Tom

</MESSAGE>

user_input:
Good morning,
We are extending the duration of our team’s RCS and RDS licenses for one more year.
I have already obtained a quote for duration extension of our RCS storage (storage-6063) from the Self Service. This is good.
I would like to now request a bespoke quote for duration extension of our RDS licence that cannot be generated via the Self Service.
We have paid for 700TB of RDS until 21 April 2026 [https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/project/114/](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/project/114/)
Firstly, to align our RDS with RCS expiry date, we would like to rebase this date so that they both expire on the same date.
Secondly, we only require 400TB of RDS for next year and I have already reduced our Lustre data to below 400TB.
Therefore, we need a quote for our next year’s RDS licence to look like this:
Duration Extension              Research Data Store     400 TB  Serena Nik-Zainal (sn206)       28 Feb 2026     28 Feb 2027
Could you please provide a quote for these RDS storage costs?
I believe it should cost around:
400TB*£39.36 per TB per year = £15,744
Minus 52 days between 28 Feb 2026 and 21 April 2026 for which we have paid already:
52/365*(700TB*£39.36) = £3,925
Total £15,744-£3,925 = £11,819
Once I have received your final quote, I will contact our finance team to produce a Purchase Order.
Regards
Yasin
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS Licence / Custom Quote
Technical Action Required: Yes (licence update + filesystem change)
Finance Action Required: Yes (formal quotation + PO required)
Escalation Required: Yes (Storage Services team)
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requests:
- Custom quote for RDS duration extension
- Reduction from 700TB to 400TB
- Rebase of licence expiry date
- Confirmation of cost calculation

Self Service cannot generate this quote; Storage Services must issue a formal quotation and reference.

PUBLISHED PRICING (Research Data Store)
Minimum quantity: 1TB
Minimum term: 1 year
Standard price: £54.00 per TB per year
Volume price: £39.36 per TB per year

PROVISIONAL ESTIMATE (BASED ON PUBLISHED PRICING)

400TB × £39.36 = £15,744.00

Pro-rata adjustment for 52 days already paid on 700TB:
700 × £39.36 = £27,552.00
52/365 × £27,552.00 ≈ £3,925.12

Estimated payable:
£15,744.00 − £3,925.12 ≈ £11,818.88

(Note: Minor rounding differences may occur depending on calculation method.)

ACTION

1) Escalate to Storage Services
   - Confirm revised capacity (400TB)
   - Confirm new start/end dates
   - Calculate official extension cost and pro-rata adjustment
   - Issue formal quotation with reference number

2) Await PO
   - Do not update portal or filesystem until PO is received.

3) Upon receipt of PO
   - Update storage licence in portal (capacity + duration)
   - Apply corresponding filesystem quota changes.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Yasin,

Based on the published RDS volume pricing of £39.36 per TB per year, a 400TB extension for one year would be:

400 × £39.36 = £15,744.00

Applying a pro-rata adjustment for the 52 days already paid on the existing 700TB allocation:

Estimated adjustment ≈ £3,925.12

Estimated total payable ≈ £11,818.88

Please note this is an estimate based on published pricing. A formal quotation with reference number will be issued by the Storage Services team shortly.

Kind regards,
HPC Support
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Licence Management
Technical Action Required: Yes
Portal Action Required: Yes
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user requires a bespoke quote for extending their RDS licence, which cannot be generated via the Self Service portal. They need to align the RDS expiry date with their RCS expiry date and reduce the storage capacity from 700TB to 400TB. The user has provided a calculation for the expected cost, but a formal quote is needed.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Generate a bespoke quote for the RDS licence extension as per the user's requirements:
   - Duration Extension: 28 Feb 2026 to 28 Feb 2027
   - Storage Capacity: 400TB
   - Data Owner: Serena Nik-Zainal (sn206)
   - Calculate the cost considering the prorated amount for the days already paid for (52 days between 28 Feb 2026 and 21 April 2026).
2. Provide the quote to the user for their finance team to produce a Purchase Order.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Yasin,

Thank you for your detailed request. We will generate a bespoke quote for your RDS licence extension as per your specifications:
- Duration Extension: 28 Feb 2026 to 28 Feb 2027
- Storage Capacity: 400TB
- Data Owner: Serena Nik-Zainal (sn206)

We will calculate the cost considering the prorated amount for the days already paid for (52 days between 28 Feb 2026 and 21 April 2026). Once the quote is ready, we will send it to you for your finance team to produce a Purchase Order.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure the quote is accurate and reflects the user's requirements.
- Follow the standard procedure for generating bespoke quotes for storage licence extensions.

REFERENCE KEY

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0007 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Katy,
This has come through as a reply to the earlier ticket you raised, rather than a new one.
Individual storage licenses over 100TB are priced using the volume rates, rather than per PI. So I'm afraid that renewing both of these would be at the standard rate.
I've reduced the size of the rds-aef24-virusbioinformat project down to 50TB in the portal and on disk, so it will be showing as over quota currently. You can generate a quote to extend this account by 12 months (from 1 Jan 2023) at the link below using the 'Extend My Storage Duration' link at the top of the page;
[https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/account/112/](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/account/112/)
50TB for 12 months should be £2,700.00, which would then need a purchase order submitted in the portal against the online quote, addressed to 'Information Services'.
Extending the validity of the rcs-aef24-virusbioinformat project and increasing it's size will have to be done in two steps, with two purchase orders. That's down to the fact that the cost to increase the size does vary, depending on when you make the purchase. I.e. if the account ends on 31 Dec 2023, then adding 30TB today to last till then would be for 11 months and 11 days, if you made the purchase next Friday it would be for 11 months and 4 days,  etc.
Extending the RCS account at the existing 30TB level by 12 months would be £921.60, the quote can be generated at;
[https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/account/288/](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/account/288/)
Once the PO for that is submitted, you can generate a new quote on the same page to extend it by a further 30TB.
Hopefully that makes sense, but just let me know if you need any assistance or advice.
Regards
Leon
Research Computing Services

</MESSAGE>

2. Yes, if you increase the size and extend it in two separate purchases.
Regards
Leon
Research Computing Services

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0015 role=REPORTER>
Dear Leon,
Thank you for your reply.
I have increased our RDS storage limit and the license was for 5 years.
For our RCS however, I had to first increase the size to 15TB which will take  place from 18th of July. In the meantime, I have created two quotes to extend the date for two additional years. Can you please confirm that I am doing this right? So we have 15TB until June 2023, and we would like to have 15TB until June 2025. Please find the two separate quotes attached. Once I hear back from you, I will create purchase orders for them.
Many thanks again for your help! 
Best wishes
Marjan
[FILE ATTACHMENT]
 (56 kB)
[FILE ATTACHMENT]
 (56 kB)

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0016 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Marjan,
Yes, 15TB of storage for 12 months comes to £460.80 - (15 x £30.72).
[https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/research-data-storage-services/price-list](https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/research-data-storage-services/price-list)
So it would cost that amount each time you extend by 12 months.
Regards
Leon
Research Computing Services

user_input:
Good morning,
We are extending the duration of our team’s RCS and RDS licenses for one more year.
I have already obtained a quote for duration extension of our RCS storage (storage-6063) from the Self Service. This is good.
I would like to now request a bespoke quote for duration extension of our RDS licence that cannot be generated via the Self Service.
We have paid for 700TB of RDS until 21 April 2026 [https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/project/114/](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/project/114/)
Firstly, to align our RDS with RCS expiry date, we would like to rebase this date so that they both expire on the same date.
Secondly, we only require 400TB of RDS for next year and I have already reduced our Lustre data to below 400TB.
Therefore, we need a Purchase Order so that our next year’s RDS licence would look like this:
Duration Extension              Research Data Store     400 TB  Serena Nik-Zainal (sn206)       28 Feb 2026     28 Feb 2027
I believe it should cost around:
400TB*£39.36 per TB per year = £15,744
Minus 52 days between 28 Feb 2026 and 21 April 2026 for which we have paid already:
52/365*(700TB*£39.36) = £3,925
Total £15,744-£3,925 = £11,819
Could you kindly produce this Purchase Order for us?
Regards
Yasin
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS Licence / Procurement (PO)
Technical Action Required: No
Finance Action Required: Yes (user departmental finance raises PO)
Escalation Required: Yes (storage reduction + bespoke quote request to Storage Services)
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is asking HPC Support to produce a Purchase Order for an RDS duration extension and storage reduction. HPC Support cannot raise departmental purchase orders because we do not have access to the user’s funding points. The user must request the PO via their department finance team. The storage reduction / bespoke RDS quote request should be submitted to support@hpc.cam.ac.uk for escalation to the relevant Storage Services team.

ACTION
- Explain we cannot produce POs on behalf of users.
- Tell user to contact their departmental finance team to raise the PO.
- Tell user to submit the storage reduction / bespoke RDS quote request to support@hpc.cam.ac.uk so it can be escalated.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Yasin,

We do not produce purchase orders for users of our services, as we do not have access to the funding points used for departmental purchase orders. You will need to contact your finance team within your department to raise the PO.

Please submit your enquiry for reducing storage to support@hpc.cam.ac.uk so we can escalate it to the relevant team.

Best regards,
HPC Support
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Licence Management
Technical Action Required: Yes
Portal Action Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user requires a bespoke quote for an RDS licence extension that cannot be generated via the Self Service Portal. They need to align the RDS expiry date with their RCS expiry date and reduce the RDS capacity from 700TB to 400TB [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the current RDS licence details and expiry date.
2. Calculate the cost for the requested duration extension and capacity reduction.
3. Generate a bespoke quote for the user based on the verified details.
4. Provide the quote to the user for generating a Purchase Order.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Yasin,

Thank you for your request. We will generate a bespoke quote for the RDS licence extension as per your specifications. Please allow us some time to verify the current licence details and calculate the cost.

We will provide you with the quote as soon as possible.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the bespoke quote is accurate and aligns with the user's requirements.
- Follow the standard procedure for generating bespoke quotes for storage licence extensions.

REFERENCE KEY

<MESSAGE id=0007 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Katy,
This has come through as a reply to the earlier ticket you raised, rather than a new one.
Individual storage licenses over 100TB are priced using the volume rates, rather than per PI. So I'm afraid that renewing both of these would be at the standard rate.
I've reduced the size of the rds-aef24-virusbioinformat project down to 50TB in the portal and on disk, so it will be showing as over quota currently. You can generate a quote to extend this account by 12 months (from 1 Jan 2023) at the link below using the 'Extend My Storage Duration' link at the top of the page;
[https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/account/112/](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/account/112/)
50TB for 12 months should be £2,700.00, which would then need a purchase order submitted in the portal against the online quote, addressed to 'Information Services'.
Extending the validity of the rcs-aef24-virusbioinformat project and increasing it's size will have to be done in two steps, with two purchase orders. That's down to the fact that the cost to increase the size does vary, depending on when you make the purchase. I.e. if the account ends on 31 Dec 2023, then adding 30TB today to last till then would be for 11 months and 11 days, if you made the purchase next Friday it would be for 11 months and 4 days,  etc.
Extending the RCS account at the existing 30TB level by 12 months would be £921.60, the quote can be generated at;
[https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/account/288/](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/account/288/)
Once the PO for that is submitted, you can generate a new quote on the same page to extend it by a further 30TB.
Hopefully that makes sense, but just let me know if you need any assistance or advice.
Regards
Leon
Research Computing Services

</MESSAGE>

user_input:
Dear HPC,
I switched phone but did not port the MFA information to my new device.
Would you be able to help me reset this?
Many thanks,
Bart.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / MFA (TOTP) / Reset
Technical Action Required: Possibly (staff-side MFA reset after identity verification)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (access blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User switched phone and no longer has their MFA codes. First check whether they can transfer authenticator codes from the old phone. If not, MFA reset is possible but requires identity verification (quick video call + photo ID). After reset, user must re-enrol by logging in (SSH and/or web) to obtain a new QR code.

ACTION

1) Ask if they still have the old phone
   - If yes: advise transferring codes using vendor guidance:
     - Microsoft Authenticator backup/restore
     - Google Authenticator transfer/export

2) If transfer not possible: arrange MFA reset with identity verification
   - Set up a short MS Teams video call (≤ 5 minutes)
   - User presents photo ID for verification
   - Offer times within service desk hours (Mon–Fri 9am–5pm, excl. holidays)

3) After reset: instruct user how to obtain a new QR / TOTP
   - SSH method:
     ssh <CRSid>@multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk
     Enter UIS/Raven password when prompted
     Scan the displayed QR code into authenticator app
     Expect a token labelled similar to “CSD3:SSH Login”
   - Web portal method:
     Log in as normal; a QR code will be shown to enrol
     Expect a token labelled similar to “CSD3:<CRSid>”

4) Provide official MFA documentation link

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Bart,

Have you still got your old phone? If so, you may be able to transfer the authenticator codes to your new phone.
- Microsoft Authenticator: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/account-billing/back-up-account-credentials-in-microsoft-authenticator-bb939936-7a8d-4e88-bc43-49bc1a700a40
- Google Authenticator: https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/1066447?hl=en&co=GENIE.Platform%3DAndroid

If you can’t transfer the codes over, we can reset your MFA. To do so we’ll need a quick video call (no more than 5 minutes) where you can present photo ID for verification. Please let me know a suitable time and date (Service Desk hours are 9am–5pm, Mon–Fri, excluding holidays) and I can send an MS Teams invite.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not reset MFA without identity verification.
- Do not request sensitive documents over email; verification should be done via the agreed video call process.
- Do not claim MFA has been reset unless the staff-side action has been completed.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Security / MFA Reset
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user has changed phones and needs to reset their MFA. This is a common request and can be handled by the helpdesk [1][2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the user's identity through standard procedures.
2. Reset the MFA for the user's account.
3. Provide instructions to the user on how to set up MFA on their new device.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Bart,

We can assist you with resetting your MFA. Please follow these steps:

1. Verify your identity by providing your full name and date of birth.
2. Once verified, we will reset your MFA.
3. You will then receive instructions on how to set up MFA on your new device.

Please let us know if you have any further questions.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Always verify the user's identity before resetting MFA.
- Provide clear instructions to the user on how to set up MFA on their new device.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-52009::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-55928::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-80787::chunk::0000
[4] : HPCSSUP-65941::chunk::0000
[5] : HPCSSUP-49603::chunk::0000

user_input:
Hi HPC Support Team,
I’m writing to ask about a discrepancy I’ve noticed between the reported quota usage and the disk usage shown by du on our RCS space.
When I check the usage for rcs1 (rcs-zz485-sem-lab-cold) using quota, it reports approximately 1.17 TB in use. However, when I run du -h on the same RCS directory, it reports a total size of around 29 TB. In addition, I can confirm that my own subdirectory alone (/woody) is significantly larger than the ~45 GB indicated by the quota output.
This has made us a bit concerned about: whether all data on the RCS space is being correctly accounted for by the quota system, and whether there is any issue with how usage is being reported.
Could you please advise on why there might be, and whether this is expected behaviour for RCS storage?
Many thanks for your help.
All the best,
Woody
[cid:2e4c979f-7f13-4964-85b8-9b8614326b0f@GBRP265.PROD.OUTLOOK.COM]
[cid:e1468c0f-87a3-40d2-99da-259426e89246@GBRP265.PROD.OUTLOOK.COM]
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RCS / Quota Reporting
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports discrepancy between:
- `quota` showing ~1.17 TB used
- `du -h` showing ~29 TB
- Their own subdirectory appearing much larger than the quota-reported figure

On RCS (Research Cold Store), this behaviour is expected. RCS is tape-backed cold storage, and quota reporting is not updated/cached in real time due to the nature of the underlying system. Additionally, `du` by default reports allocated block usage, whereas quota on RCS reflects the apparent file size accounted by the storage system.

The correct way to estimate logical usage on RCS is to use:

    du --apparent-size

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Clarify storage type
   - Confirm the path is on RCS (e.g. /rcs/project/...).
   - Explain that RCS behaves differently from RDS or /home.

2) Explain expected discrepancy
   - RCS is tape-backed cold storage.
   - Quota values are not continuously refreshed.
   - `du -h` reports on-disk block usage.
   - Sparse files, compression, and tape staging can cause differences.

3) Provide correct diagnostic command
   Ask the user to run:
     du -sh --apparent-size /rcs/project/<project-name>
   or for a subdirectory:
     du -sh --apparent-size /rcs/project/<project-name>/woody

4) Reassure
   - This does not indicate data loss.
   - This does not indicate unaccounted storage.
   - This is expected behaviour for RCS.

5) Close once confirmed resolved.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Please confirm the exact RCS path you are checking.
- Please share the output of:
    quota
    du -sh /rcs/project/<project>
    du -sh --apparent-size /rcs/project/<project>

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Quota vs Disk Usage on RCS

Hello Woody,

Thank you for your message.

This behaviour is expected on RCS. Because RCS is tape-backed cold storage, quota information is not updated in real time, and the `quota` command does not always reflect the apparent size reported by standard `du`.

To obtain a figure that more closely matches the logical file size used for quota purposes, please run:

  du -sh --apparent-size /rcs/project/rcs-zz485-sem-lab-cold

You can also apply this to individual subdirectories (e.g. your `/woody` directory).

The discrepancy you are seeing does not indicate missing or unaccounted data — it is a result of how RCS storage and quota reporting operate.

Please let us know if you have any further concerns.

Best regards,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply quota malfunction unless confirmed by storage administrators.
- Do not suggest data loss without evidence.
- Avoid recommending destructive commands.
- Ensure users understand that RCS differs from RDS in quota behaviour.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Quota Management
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The discrepancy between the reported quota usage and the actual disk usage on RCS storage is a known issue. The quota command often reflects the data stored in the RCS cache rather than the total storage usage, including data moved to the tape library [2]. This can lead to inaccurate reporting of storage usage.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Confirm with the user that the discrepancy is due to the difference between cached data and data on tape.
2. Advise the user on how to query the total amount of storage used, including data on tape.
3. Escalate to the storage team if the issue persists or if further investigation is required.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Woody,

Thank you for bringing this to our attention. The discrepancy you're seeing between the quota usage and the actual disk usage on your RCS space is a known issue. The quota command often reflects the data stored in the RCS cache rather than the total storage usage, including data moved to the tape library [2].

To get a more accurate picture of your total storage usage, you may need to query the storage system directly. We can assist you with this if needed.

Please let us know if you have any further questions or if you need additional assistance.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user is aware of the difference between cached data and data on tape when interpreting quota usage.
- Escalate to the storage team if the issue cannot be resolved through standard procedures.

REFERENCE KEY
[2] : HPCSSUP-88989::chunk::0000

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Performance Issues with RCS File Transfers & Storage Reporting

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear HPC Storage Team,
I hope this email finds you well. I am writing about two issues with my RCS storage (/rcs1).
I have noticed extremely slow file transfer speeds when moving files from RCS to RDS, specifically in these directories:
- /rcs/user/yz720/rcs-yz720-rabbits/Joel_ancient/00_raw/
- /rcs/user/yz720/rcs-yz720-rabbits/modern_on_CIBIO/00_raw/
Additionally, there seems to be a storage reporting discrepancy for those files. When using 'du -sh *', these directories show only 512 bytes per file, which seems incorrect. This suggests the 'quota' command may not be accurately reflecting actual storage usage.
I would greatly appreciate your help investigating these issues. Please let me know if you need any additional information. Thank you for your time and assistance.
Best regards,
Yexin

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Quota usage of RCS

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear HPC support,
I’ve noticed that when running the ‘quota’ command, the usage reported for our RCS reflects only the data stored in the RCS cache, and that files once moved from the RCS cache to the tape library all have a file size of 512 bytes.
Is there a way to query the total amount of storage we’re using of our paid/allocated RCS quota?
Best,
Dr. Scott C. Ritchie
Senior Research Associate | PHPC Data Manager for CSD3
Cardiovascular Epidemiology Unit,
Department of Public Health and Primary Care,
University of Cambridge.

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Incorrect quota issue 

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hello,
Recently, one of my scripts generated a very large file, approximately 620GB in size, which resulted in my quota exceeding the allocated limit. Recognizing this, I deleted the file; however, it has been over 30 minutes since this action, and there appears to be no change reflected in my quota when I check it.
/rds-d7             1208.3     1099.5    1209.5           6d16h44m48s   619102  1048576  1048576       - P:15831
I have thoroughly reviewed all my folders within the HPC environment to verify the current usage and found that the sizes do not correspond with what is being reported in the quota terminal output. This discrepancy is hindering my ability to perform necessary analyses, as I am still subject to the quota limit despite having deleted the large file.
Understanding the time-sensitive nature of HPC work, I am reaching out to request your urgent assistance in resolving this matter. Any guidance or intervention you can provide to ensure the quota accurately reflects my current usage would be immensely appreciated.
Best,
Marcella
_______________________
Marcella Montagnese, PhD
(Personal pronouns: she/her)
Junior Research Fellow in Biological & Medical Sciences, Christ's College
Research Associate in Neuroinformatics
Department of Clinical Neurosciences
University of Cambridge
mm2075@cam.ac.uk<[mm2075@cam.ac.uk](mailto:mm2075@cam.ac.uk)>  | marcella.montagnese@nhs.net<[marcella.montagnese@nhs.net](mailto:marcella.montagnese@nhs.net)> |
@m_montagnese<[https://twitter.com/m_montagnese](https://twitter.com/m_montagnese)> | marcellamontagnese.com<[https://www.marcellamontagnese.com/](https://www.marcellamontagnese.com/)> |
Responses to emails are only expected during your working hours.

[TICKET_SUMMARY] RDS quota issue

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear HPC Support team,
Re: RDS at [https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/project/444/](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/project/444/)
Directory: rds-kgcs2-smith-lab
Project ID: CBhaM9mB5fs
[Ken Smith is no longer at the institute]
Today I received an email saying:
"According to our systems, the project is using 24TB against the 20TB
quota and I appreciate that you may not have been aware of this until
now but could I ask if you or a member of your team can check whether
all the stored data is still required and then either;
Reduce the amount held to below the 20TB limit, or
Purchase an increase in the quota to cover the additional usage."
which I understand and believe, but this is at odds with the reported usage according to the HPC-provided 'quota' command:
/rds-d2       18895.2    20000.0   20000.0      -  1537557 10240000 10240000       - G:rds-kgcs2-smith-lab
– I make that 18.9Tb out of 20Tb.
and: I've been using it regularly for the last few weeks, and specifically monitoring usage as I've been running resource-heavy nextflow pipelines. It's been under 18Tb the whole time.
There is no 'early warning' system, via email to managers (us) or even the command-line.
This is somewhat frustrating. It seems that we aren't able to get an accurate picture of the usage on our own disk storage.
Can you advise please?
Regards,
Kelvin Hunter
Data Manager,
Dept of Medicine

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Re: CSD3 storage project over quota - rcs-pal34-smithlab-archive

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear Leon
Thanks for bringing this to our attention. We will address this asap.
Best wishes,
Paul

I'm helping someone with an issue which has involved the `ukaea-icl-himem` nodes and I noticed that it seems that the nodes for that partition ( `cpu-q-[429-440,442-444]` ) are also in the general `ukaea-icl` pool (see below). This has meant that although their empty I'm not able to test the issue on that partition. I just wanted to make sure this is the expected setup?
`[ir-swan1@login-p-1 jez_scripts]$ sinfo -p ukaea-icl-himem`
`PARTITION       AVAIL  TIMELIMIT  NODES  STATE NODELIST`
`ukaea-icl-himem    up 7-00:00:00      1  maint cpu-q-441`
`ukaea-icl-himem    up 7-00:00:00     15  alloc cpu-q-[429-440,442-444]`
`[ir-swan1@login-p-1 jez_scripts]$ squeue --format "%i,%20P,%j" -w cpu-q-[429-440,442-444]`
`JOBID,PARTITION           ,NAME`
`21455318,ukaea-icl           ,050m0400`
`21455285,ukaea-icl           ,050m0400`
`21455303,ukaea-icl           ,050m0400`
`21455282,ukaea-icl           ,050m0400`
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / Partition Configuration / Scheduler Policy
Technical Action Required: No (configuration clarification only)
Escalation Required: No (unless policy change requested)
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User observes that nodes in the `ukaea-icl-himem` partition (cpu-q-[429-440,442-444]) are also appearing in the general `ukaea-icl` partition. As a result, although `ukaea-icl-himem` appears empty, the nodes are running jobs from the general partition, preventing immediate testing.

This configuration is intentional.

The higher-memory nodes are shared between:
- `ukaea-icl-himem` (higher memory per CPU requirement)
- `ukaea-icl` (general partition)

The scheduler is configured so that:
- Himem nodes may run jobs from the general partition when no himem jobs are waiting.
- If himem jobs are queued, the scheduler will prioritise them appropriately.
- Nodes may therefore be busy with general jobs even if the himem queue is empty at that moment.

This kind of overlap is used to improve utilisation rather than leaving higher-memory nodes idle.

ACTION

1) Confirm configuration is intentional.
2) Explain scheduler behaviour clearly:
   - Himem nodes are not reserved exclusively.
   - They backfill general jobs when idle.
   - Himem jobs will be prioritised when submitted.
3) Do not suggest misconfiguration unless confirmed by cluster admins.
4) Escalate only if the user is requesting a policy/configuration change rather than clarification.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: ukaea-icl-himem partition behaviour

Hello Jez,

The overlap between `ukaea-icl-himem` and `ukaea-icl` is intentional and mirrors how the Cambridge icelake/icelake-himem partitions are configured.

The higher-memory nodes are allowed to run jobs from the general `ukaea-icl` partition when there are no himem jobs waiting. This avoids leaving those nodes idle. If there is demand for the himem partition, the scheduler will prioritise those jobs accordingly.

This means that although the himem queue may appear empty at a given moment, the nodes can still be occupied by general jobs and you may need to wait for those jobs to complete before himem resources become available.

I hope this clarifies the setup.

Best regards,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply scheduler misconfiguration without confirmation.
- Do not promise dedicated reservation of himem nodes unless policy changes are approved.
- Avoid recommending workarounds that bypass scheduling policy.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: HPC / Node Allocation
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user has observed that nodes in the `ukaea-icl-himem` partition are also allocated to the general `ukaea-icl` pool, which is preventing them from testing an issue on that partition. This setup may be intentional, but it requires verification [1][2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the current configuration of the `ukaea-icl-himem` and `ukaea-icl` partitions.
2. Check if the shared allocation of nodes between these partitions is intentional and documented.
3. If the configuration is incorrect, escalate to the appropriate team for correction.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact error message or issue you are encountering when trying to test on the `ukaea-icl-himem` partition?
- Have you tried submitting jobs to other nodes in the `ukaea-icl-himem` partition?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,

Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We will verify the current configuration of the `ukaea-icl-himem` and `ukaea-icl` partitions to ensure that the shared allocation of nodes is intentional. If there is an issue, we will escalate it to the appropriate team for correction.

Please provide the exact error message or issue you are encountering when trying to test on the `ukaea-icl-himem` partition, and let us know if you have tried submitting jobs to other nodes in that partition.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that any changes to node allocations are documented and communicated to affected users.
- Do not make unauthorized changes to partition configurations without proper verification and approval.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-59451::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-89019::chunk::0002

[TICKET_SUMMARY] epid partition problem - nodes also allocated to cclake?

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hi,
My colleague Isobel asked me about her jobs so I took a look and I wonder if there is a configuration problem?
She reported that her jobs were still queued, I checked on the queue and found that all jobs were pending and none running:
squeue | grep epid
JOBID PARTITION     NAME     USER ST       TIME  NODES NODELIST(REASON)
24270455_[2]      epid    SuSIE    ids32 PD       0:00      1 (Priority)
24270425      epid build38p    ids32 PD       0:00      1 (Resources)
24149839      epid bolt_bio   am2037 PD       0:00      1 (launch failed requeued held)
24149838      epid bolt_bio   am2037 PD       0:00      1 (launch failed requeued held)
So I checked on the partitions using sinfo and it looks like the epid nodes are all allocated despite no jobs running. The epid nodes are cpu-p[157-159] and it looks like those same nodes are allocated to cclake*
PARTITION     AVAIL  TIMELIMIT  NODES  STATE NODELIST
epid             up   infinite      3  alloc cpu-p-[157-159]
cclake*          up 7-00:00:00    296  alloc cpu-p-[78-84,123-154,157-166,169,171-180,186,188-200,207,212,214-215,226,233-254,288-320,327-328,330-341,345-355,359,361-362,366-368,401,404,409-412,417-500,505-512,546-553,555-560,589,591-598,601-605,607-609,611-612]
I'm still new to this so I may have misunderstood but could you please look into it?
Thanks,
Nicola
________________________________
From: Isobel Stewart <Isobel.Stewart@mrc-epid.cam.ac.uk>
Sent: Thursday, July 27, 2023 3:02 PM
To: Nicola Kerrison <Nicola.Kerrison@mrc-epid.cam.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: HPC downtime & job submission changes on Tue/Wed
Hi Nicola,
Do you know if anyone has successfully submitted things to hpc?
My jobs have been waiting for a few hours now. Im using the account and project below.
#SBATCH -A MRC-EPID-SL0-CPU
#SBATCH -p epid
Thanks,
Isobel
From: Nicola Kerrison <Nicola.Kerrison@mrc-epid.cam.ac.uk>
Sent: 20 July 2023 13:06
To: EPID genetics <genetics@mrc-epid.cam.ac.uk>
Subject: HPC downtime & job submission changes on Tue/Wed
Dear all,
University HPC, storage services RDS, RFS and RCS and our secure platform SCRP will be down Tuesday and Wednesday 25-26 July and we are asked to avoid trying to reach them or contacting the service desk for these two days. All Skylake nodes will be retired on Monday 24 July at 0900. If this comes as a surprise, do remember to skim read your emails that are sent to hpc-user-request@lists.cam.ac.uk<[hpc-user-request@lists.cam.ac.uk](mailto:hpc-user-request@lists.cam.ac.uk)> or let hpc support know that you need to join this list.
Information will be communicated on:
[https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/full_maintenance-Jul2023.html](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/full_maintenance-Jul2023.html)
Following this, there will be changes to our current compute nodes that you need to be aware of.
If you currently use the dedicated nodes queue: MRC-EPID-SL0-CPU:
Our current "epid" nodes have reached their end of life and are being retired. We had 30 individual nodes each with 20 cores and 256GB of RAM. John is currently planning on a replacement but for the next four months HPC have allocated us have been allocated 3x of their “Cascade Lake” nodes (each with 56 cores and 192GB RAM), giving us free, dedicated use of a combined 168 cores – each core has 3.43GB RAM available and is much more performant in terms of raw CPU power than those in our old partition. HPC tell us that this is actually more than our average use over the last 12 months.
Please discuss with the wider HPC user group in the unit if you plan to run large jobs for extended periods of time, and consider carefully whether you need to ask for exclusive use of the node.
We currently understand that the account and project for job submission will remain the same, it will just go automatically to the new nodes.
If you currently use other queues:
If you were submitting to the "skylake" partition, eg if your submission script or command contains "-p skylake" you will need to update your script to use a different partition - HPC support recommend cascade lake "-p cclake" as a replacement.
For all:
The memory available per CPU has decreased on the new SL0 queue, and on cclake relative to skylake. If you need more memory for your job you will need to request more cores for your job. You can use the "seff" command to check what resources your job actually used to refine your requests.
Essentially, If you are currently using the MRC-EPID-SL0-CPU queue or any skylake partition (if your submission script contains "-p skylake" you were), the CPUs you are running on will need to change after the downtime. The architecture of the new nodes is slightly different and there is a possibility that some things may need to be recompiled. Brian Lam has some knowledge on this and may be able to help, but ask the genetics email list first.
If you have any problems, you can use the genetics email list to discuss and ask for help in case anyone in the unit has already solved your problem.
Best wishes,

[TICKET_SUMMARY] epid partition problem - nodes also allocated to cclake?

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hi,
My colleague Isobel asked me about her jobs so I took a look and I wonder if there is a configuration problem?
She reported that her jobs were still queued, I checked on the queue and found that all jobs were pending and none running:
squeue | grep epid
JOBID PARTITION     NAME     USER ST       TIME  NODES NODELIST(REASON)
24270455_[2]      epid    SuSIE    ids32 PD       0:00      1 (Priority)
24270425      epid build38p    ids32 PD       0:00      1 (Resources)
24149839      epid bolt_bio   am2037 PD       0:00      1 (launch failed requeued held)
24149838      epid bolt_bio   am2037 PD       0:00      1 (launch failed requeued held)
So I checked on the partitions using sinfo and it looks like the epid nodes are all allocated despite no jobs running. The epid nodes are cpu-p[157-159] and it looks like those same nodes are allocated to cclake*
PARTITION     AVAIL  TIMELIMIT  NODES  STATE NODELIST
epid             up   infinite      3  alloc cpu-p-[157-159]
cclake*          up 7-00:00:00    296  alloc cpu-p-[78-84,123-154,157-166,169,171-180,186,188-200,207,212,214-215,226,233-254,288-320,327-328,330-341,345-355,359,361-362,366-368,401,404,409-412,417-500,505-512,546-553,555-560,589,591-598,601-605,607-609,611-612]
I'm still new to this so I may have misunderstood but could you please look into it?
Thanks,
Nicola
________________________________
From: Isobel Stewart <Isobel.Stewart@mrc-epid.cam.ac.uk>
Sent: Thursday, July 27, 2023 3:02 PM
To: Nicola Kerrison <Nicola.Kerrison@mrc-epid.cam.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: HPC downtime & job submission changes on Tue/Wed
Hi Nicola,
Do you know if anyone has successfully submitted things to hpc?
My jobs have been waiting for a few hours now. Im using the account and project below.
#SBATCH -A MRC-EPID-SL0-CPU
#SBATCH -p epid
Thanks,
Isobel
From: Nicola Kerrison <Nicola.Kerrison@mrc-epid.cam.ac.uk>
Sent: 20 July 2023 13:06
To: EPID genetics <genetics@mrc-epid.cam.ac.uk>
Subject: HPC downtime & job submission changes on Tue/Wed
Dear all,
University HPC, storage services RDS, RFS and RCS and our secure platform SCRP will be down Tuesday and Wednesday 25-26 July and we are asked to avoid trying to reach them or contacting the service desk for these two days. All Skylake nodes will be retired on Monday 24 July at 0900. If this comes as a surprise, do remember to skim read your emails that are sent to hpc-user-request@lists.cam.ac.uk<[hpc-user-request@lists.cam.ac.uk](mailto:hpc-user-request@lists.cam.ac.uk)> or let hpc support know that you need to join this list.
Information will be communicated on:
[https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/full_maintenance-Jul2023.html](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/full_maintenance-Jul2023.html)
Following this, there will be changes to our current compute nodes that you need to be aware of.
If you currently use the dedicated nodes queue: MRC-EPID-SL0-CPU:
Our current "epid" nodes have reached their end of life and are being retired. We had 30 individual nodes each with 20 cores and 256GB of RAM. John is currently planning on a replacement but for the next four months HPC have allocated us have been allocated 3x of their “Cascade Lake” nodes (each with 56 cores and 192GB RAM), giving us free, dedicated use of a combined 168 cores – each core has 3.43GB RAM available and is much more performant in terms of raw CPU power than those in our old partition. HPC tell us that this is actually more than our average use over the last 12 months.
Please discuss with the wider HPC user group in the unit if you plan to run large jobs for extended periods of time, and consider carefully whether you need to ask for exclusive use of the node.
We currently understand that the account and project for job submission will remain the same, it will just go automatically to the new nodes.
If you currently use other queues:
If you were submitting to the "skylake" partition, eg if your submission script or command contains "-p skylake" you will need to update your script to use a different partition - HPC support recommend cascade lake "-p cclake" as a replacement.
For all:
The memory available per CPU has decreased on the new SL0 queue, and on cclake relative to skylake. If you need more memory for your job you will need to request more cores for your job. You can use the "seff" command to check what resources your job actually used to refine your requests.
Essentially, If you are currently using the MRC-EPID-SL0-CPU queue or any skylake partition (if your submission script contains "-p skylake" you were), the CPUs you are running on will need to change after the downtime. The architecture of the new nodes is slightly different and there is a possibility that some things may need to be recompiled. Brian Lam has some knowledge on this and may be able to help, but ask the genetics email list first.
If you have any problems, you can use the genetics email list to discuss and ask for help in case anyone in the unit has already solved your problem.
Best wishes,
Nicola
