user_input:
Hi, 
I wonder if I can get some advice. 
I am one of the data managers for the project rds-HvBBlpwx4dc, and need some advice on the best way to manage the file permissions. 
The space is used for a number of projects that our group actively work on, however when trying the manage the space and run commands such as du -sh /rds/project/rds-HvBBlpwx4dc/ I get a lot of permission denied for folders. 
In addition to this if new members of the group starts I cannot easily give them access to group data if the file was generated by a different member of the group and have to result in asking that user to run commands on my behalf to adjust the file permissions. 
Whats the best way to mitigate this issue ?
I look forward to hearing from you 
Best wishes
James
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS / Permissions & ACLs
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No (unless ACLs/groups are misconfigured)
Urgency: Low–Medium (group collaboration friction)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is a data manager for RDS project rds-HvBBlpwx4dc and reports:

1) Running:
   du -sh /rds/project/rds-HvBBlpwx4dc/
   results in multiple “Permission denied” errors.

2) Difficulty granting access to new group members because files created by one user are not automatically accessible to others.

This indicates standard Unix permission behaviour combined with missing or inconsistent default ACL configuration.

RDS projects are expected to use:
- Project-level Unix groups (e.g. rds-*-managers / rds-*-users)
- POSIX ACLs (setfacl) to enforce consistent group access
- Default ACLs (d:) to ensure newly created files inherit correct permissions

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm project group structure
   - Verify the user is in the appropriate project groups:
       id
   - Typical structure:
       rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-managers
       rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-users

2) Explain why "Permission denied" appears in du
   - If subdirectories are owned by individuals with restrictive permissions (e.g. 700),
     other project members cannot traverse them.
   - du attempts to enter all directories and reports errors when traversal fails.

3) Recommend standard RDS permission model
   Refer user to:
   https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rds/permissions.html

   Explain that project directories should:
   - Be group-owned by the appropriate rds-* group
   - Have group execute bit set on directories (required for traversal)

4) Provide ACL-based solution (recommended approach)

   To grant group read/execute on an existing directory tree:
       setfacl -R -m g:rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-users:rx /rds/project/rds-HvBBlpwx4dc

   To ensure new files and directories inherit correct permissions:
       setfacl -R -d -m g:rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-users:rx /rds/project/rds-HvBBlpwx4dc

   Clarify:
       The extra `d:` sets the *default ACL*.
       This ensures that newly created items inherit the correct group permissions.

5) Optional: set default directory mask (if appropriate)
   Users may also ensure directories are created with group execute bit enabled
   (e.g. via umask 002 within collaborative groups).

6) Do NOT:
   - Suggest 777 permissions.
   - Suggest changing ownership unless necessary.
   - Escalate unless group membership is incorrect at system level.

IF PROBLEM PERSISTS

- Check actual ACLs:
    getfacl /rds/project/rds-HvBBlpwx4dc
- Confirm directory group ownership:
    ls -ld /rds/project/rds-HvBBlpwx4dc
- Verify new member is in correct Unix group.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: File permission management on RDS project

Hello James,

On RDS projects we recommend following the permissions model described here:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rds/permissions.html

In general, project members should be in either the rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-managers or rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-users group so that access is controlled at the group level rather than per individual user.

To ensure that new files and directories automatically inherit the correct group permissions, you can set a default ACL on the project directory, for example:

  setfacl -R -d -m g:rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-users:rx /rds/project/rds-HvBBlpwx4dc

The extra `d:` sets the default ACL, which applies to newly created items and ensures they inherit the correct group permissions.

You may also need to update existing files/directories using:

  setfacl -R -m g:rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-users:rx /rds/project/rds-HvBBlpwx4dc

This approach should prevent the need to manually adjust permissions each time a new group member joins.

Please let us know if you would like us to check the current ACLs on the project directory.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Avoid recommending overly permissive modes (e.g. 777).
- Default ACLs are the preferred collaborative model on RDS.
- Ensure group-based access rather than per-user manual fixes.
- Only escalate if Unix group membership is incorrect at system level.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Permissions Management
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing permission issues with their RDS project space, which is affecting their ability to manage the space and grant access to new members. This is a common issue when files and folders are created by different users with varying permissions [1][2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the current permissions and group ownership of the problematic folders using `ls -l` and `getfacl` commands.
2. If necessary, adjust the permissions and group ownership to ensure that all data managers have the required access. This may involve using `chown` and `chmod` commands [3].
3. Advise the user on setting default permissions for new files and folders to avoid future issues. This can be done using the `setfacl` command to set default ACLs [1][2].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the output of `ls -l` and `getfacl` for the problematic folders?
- Have you tried using the `setfacl` command to set default permissions for new files and folders?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello James,

Thank you for your message. The issue you're experiencing is common when files and folders are created by different users with varying permissions. We will need to verify the current permissions and group ownership of the problematic folders.

Could you please provide the output of the following commands for the problematic folders:
- `ls -l`
- `getfacl`

This will help us understand the current state and make the necessary adjustments. Additionally, we can advise you on setting default permissions for new files and folders to avoid future issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that any changes to permissions and group ownership are made carefully to avoid disrupting access for other users [1][2][3].
- Always verify the current state of permissions before making changes [3].

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Permissions Issue

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hi, 
I am one of the data managers for the rds-zhao-group-HvBBlpwx4dc RDS storage.
When the licence was first set up a number of users were added as data managers, however we have tried to optimise this, and made all but 2 people users. 
However some users have seemed to made folders/files within this storage space that do not have the rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-managers as the group on the linux permissions. 
They also have set some of the folders permissions to "drwx--S---+  3 zh417 rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-managers” which means I am unable to access the folder. 
Is there a way that you can change the permissions on all the files/folders within the rds-zhao-group-HvBBlpwx4dc to ensure that the rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-managers have RWX access?
Best Wishes
James

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Update access to project folder

[CONTEXT]
niversityofcambridgecloud.onmicrosoft.com%7C064c33eea7c346a8a61408dae014e6f9%7C49a50445bdfa4b79ade3547b4f3986e9%7C1%7C0%7C638068677619382717%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=OL4L0mAuqjVXeMeiS1QHbtm45rUio1y%2BO%2BbB6%2Fa%2BkHs%3D&reserved=0)

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0006 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Pedro,
The permissions on the folder are correct, it should be 770. It's the group ownership that your manager needs to change. They should run: "chown -R <owner_username>:rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-users /rds/project/rds-bDy7QPUmZq4/<folder_name>"
where owner_username is the CRSid of the data manager that created the folder for you and the <folder_name> is the name of the folder. Assuming the new folder is the one named Pedro, the command in this case would be: chown -R gp468:rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-users /rds/project/rds-bDy7QPUmZq4/Pedro.
By default any top level folder in the RDS storage created by a data manager is owned by the manager that created it and the rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-managers group. In order for data users (like yourself) to be able to create sub directories, copy data and delete folders/files from this newly created top level folder, the ownership of the folder must be changed to the rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-users group. You can verify this by running "ls -al" and you will see that all other folders in the storage are owned by the user that created them and the rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-users group. 
Data managers can create folders in any directory of the storage, read and write in any directory and delete any files or folders. When a data manager creates a new folder, it is owned by the manager that created it and the "managers" group. As a data user you can only create new directories in folders you have write permissions to. So in order to write to the newly created directory, the data manager needs to change the owning group of the folder to the "users" group as I explain above. Once that's done you can create new folders, write files and delete folders/files in this new directory. Any new folders you create as a data user can be read and edited by other data users and of course the data managers that can read/write to any folder in the storage. In short data managers can read/write/create/delete to any folder of the storage, data users can read/write/create/delete to any folder this user created or any folder that is owned by the rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-users group.
I hope this makes sense. Let me know if you have any other questions.
Kind regards,
Avraam

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0007a role=TICKET_CREATOR part=1/2 original_id=0007>
Dear Avraam, 
This is very useful - thank you! 
It does indeed make sense now. 
I've asked our data manager and all seems to be working now. 
Again, thank you very much. 
Happy holidays if it's the case.  
Kind regards, 
Pedro

</MESSAGE>

Dear Mark, 
  
As far as I remember data users cannot create folders in the main RDS space, but if any of the managers create a folder and then change the group owner of the folder (chgrp rds-qq9xNRCBjCY-users folder_name), they should be able to use that folder (writing access too). The problem is, when we create folders as data managers, the group is set to the managers. I created a test_folder in the RDS space and set the group to the users, I think both of them are able to use it, write into it. 
  
Of course only we (you as the owner, I am as the manager) are able to change these ownerships. 
  
Best, 
Lajos

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Permissions Issue

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hi, 
I am one of the data managers for the rds-zhao-group-HvBBlpwx4dc RDS storage.
When the licence was first set up a number of users were added as data managers, however we have tried to optimise this, and made all but 2 people users. 
However some users have seemed to made folders/files within this storage space that do not have the rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-managers as the group on the linux permissions. 
They also have set some of the folders permissions to "drwx--S---+  3 zh417 rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-managers” which means I am unable to access the folder. 
Is there a way that you can change the permissions on all the files/folders within the rds-zhao-group-HvBBlpwx4dc to ensure that the rds-HvBBlpwx4dc-managers have RWX access?
Best Wishes
James

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Update access to project folder

[CONTEXT]
niversityofcambridgecloud.onmicrosoft.com%7C064c33eea7c346a8a61408dae014e6f9%7C49a50445bdfa4b79ade3547b4f3986e9%7C1%7C0%7C638068677619382717%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=OL4L0mAuqjVXeMeiS1QHbtm45rUio1y%2BO%2BbB6%2Fa%2BkHs%3D&reserved=0)

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0006 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Pedro,
The permissions on the folder are correct, it should be 770. It's the group ownership that your manager needs to change. They should run: "chown -R <owner_username>:rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-users /rds/project/rds-bDy7QPUmZq4/<folder_name>"
where owner_username is the CRSid of the data manager that created the folder for you and the <folder_name> is the name of the folder. Assuming the new folder is the one named Pedro, the command in this case would be: chown -R gp468:rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-users /rds/project/rds-bDy7QPUmZq4/Pedro.
By default any top level folder in the RDS storage created by a data manager is owned by the manager that created it and the rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-managers group. In order for data users (like yourself) to be able to create sub directories, copy data and delete folders/files from this newly created top level folder, the ownership of the folder must be changed to the rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-users group. You can verify this by running "ls -al" and you will see that all other folders in the storage are owned by the user that created them and the rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-users group. 
Data managers can create folders in any directory of the storage, read and write in any directory and delete any files or folders. When a data manager creates a new folder, it is owned by the manager that created it and the "managers" group. As a data user you can only create new directories in folders you have write permissions to. So in order to write to the newly created directory, the data manager needs to change the owning group of the folder to the "users" group as I explain above. Once that's done you can create new folders, write files and delete folders/files in this new directory. Any new folders you create as a data user can be read and edited by other data users and of course the data managers that can read/write to any folder in the storage. In short data managers can read/write/create/delete to any folder of the storage, data users can read/write/create/delete to any folder this user created or any folder that is owned by the rds-bDy7QPUmZq4-users group.
I hope this makes sense. Let me know if you have any other questions.
Kind regards,
Avraam

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0007a role=TICKET_CREATOR part=1/2 original_id=0007>
Dear Avraam, 
This is very useful - thank you! 
It does indeed make sense now. 
I've asked our data manager and all seems to be working now. 
Again, thank you very much. 
Happy holidays if it's the case.  
Kind regards, 
Pedro

</MESSAGE>

I created a test_folder in the RDS space and set the group to the users, I think both of them are able to use it, write into it.

Dear Support Team,
Please could my public key be authorised for use: AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIBxSn0jwGloBiJ+etrtwLgbA37Z2ZIEXlPuaf2KTjVuu
Yours,
Ayoife Dada.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / SSH / Public Key
Technical Action Required: No (user self-service)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is an internal Cambridge account holder (CRSid email visible in ticket metadata).
Internal users authenticate via UIS/Raven and can install their own SSH public keys.

HPC Support does not manually install public keys.

ACTION

User should install their own public key using:

    ssh-copy-id <CRSid>@login.hpc.cam.ac.uk

or

    ssh-copy-id <CRSid>@multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk

They will be prompted for their UIS/Raven password. This appends the key to:

    ~/.ssh/authorized_keys


EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Ayoife,

Since you are an internal user, you can authenticate with your UIS password and install your SSH public key yourself.

From your local machine, please run:

  ssh-copy-id <CRSid>@login.hpc.cam.ac.uk

You will be prompted for your password, and this will append your key to your CSD3 account.

Please let us know if you encounter any issues.

Best wishes,
HPC Support


SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Never request or handle private keys.
- Do not accept SSH keys via email for manual installation.
- Do not manually install public keys on behalf of users; internal users must use the supported self-service method (e.g. ssh-copy-id).
response:

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

2. Authorize the public key for the user's account.
3. Confirm the authorization with the user.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Dear Ayoife Dada,

Your public key has been received and will be authorized shortly. We will confirm once the authorization is complete.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure the public key is correctly formatted and belongs to the user before authorization.
- Follow standard procedures for public key authorization as per previous tickets [1][2][3][4][5].

user_input:
Hi there,
I am attempting to download CT scan folders from the server rcs.rja50.umzc.ctdata. These folders contain approximately 3000 images each, and I am being requested to input a password before the transfer of every single file. This has happened on multiple machines and has not been an issue previously. It is delaying accessing files significantly and meaning I have to constantly attend downloads. Is there a way to fix this? Thanks in advance.
Best wishes,
Ket
Keturah Smithson (she/her), Managing Operator
Cambridge Biotomography Centre, Department of Zoology
University of Cambridge
CB2 3EJ
Tel: (01223 7) 65405
[https://www.cbc.zoo.cam.ac.uk/](https://www.cbc.zoo.cam.ac.uk/)
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Data Transfer / FileZilla / SFTP Client Behaviour
Technical Action Required: No (user workflow guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low–Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is downloading folders containing ~3000 images each and is prompted to enter a password for every individual file transfer. This is a common drawback of some GUI SFTP clients (e.g., FileZilla), which can prompt for credentials per transfer when handling many small files.

This is not evidence of an HPC authentication issue.

ACTION
Advise the user to compress many small files into a single archive (tar or zip) before downloading, then transfer the single archive instead of thousands of individual files.

Example (run on the remote server in the directory containing the CT scans):

  tar -czf ct_scans.tar.gz <ct_scan_folder>/

Then download `ct_scans.tar.gz` as a single file.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Ket,

Unfortunately this is one of the drawbacks if you are using e.g., FileZilla. You will be prompted to enter credentials for every transfer.

We normally suggest compressing the files into a tar or zip to make the transfer smoother and easier, then downloading the single archive file. For example:

  tar -czf ct_scans.tar.gz <ct_scan_folder>/

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply authentication malfunction unless there is additional evidence (e.g. failed logins elsewhere).
- Avoid recommending insecure transfer methods.
- Keep guidance aligned with supported/helpdesk-standard workflows.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Data Transfer
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing repeated password prompts while downloading CT scan folders from the server, which is causing significant delays. This issue has been reported by multiple users and has not been an issue previously [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify if there are any known issues with the server or authentication system.
2. Check if there have been any recent changes to the server configuration or authentication policies.
3. If the issue persists, escalate to the relevant technical team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Have there been any recent changes to your network or firewall settings?
- Are you using the latest version of the software or client for downloading the files?
- Can you provide the exact error message or any relevant logs?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Keturah,

Thank you for reaching out. We understand that you are experiencing repeated password prompts while downloading CT scan folders from the server, which is causing significant delays. This issue has been reported by multiple users and has not been an issue previously [1].

We will investigate this issue further and check if there are any known issues with the server or authentication system. In the meantime, please let us know if there have been any recent changes to your network or firewall settings, and if you are using the latest version of the software or client for downloading the files.

We will keep you updated on the progress and do our best to resolve this issue as soon as possible.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that any changes to the server configuration or authentication policies are communicated to users in advance.
- Follow the appropriate escalation procedures if the issue cannot be resolved by the helpdesk.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-92273::chunk::0000

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Request for help - unable to download scans

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hi CSD3 team,
My name is Elizabeth McKiernan. em654
My PI is John O’Brien
I am trying to download neuroimaging data from the rcs on csd3 using the Filezilla app. I am trying to download the data to a group hard drive (to allow multiple users who all have permission to access the data to work with it).
We are not able to perform the processing that we want on the HPC, we need to do it on our local machines.
The data is at:
/rcs/project/jto27/rcs-jto27-p00423/MRS_AnnaS_visit2/MRSv2
I can ssh into login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk using my CRIS credentials and password and the secondary authentication code via the app but only the first few scans download each time – during which time I am asked repeatedly to input my password and authentication code. Then I am shortly disconnected from the server. Other lab users have experienced the same.
I would be grateful if you could tell me what I might be doing wrong or if there is an alternative way of downloading the data we need.
Thank you for your help
Best wishes
Lizz

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Issue with MRI Data Download

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear administrator,
I'm reaching out regarding a problem that we encountered while attempting to access the data from 3T and 7T MRI's that were acquired yesterday. I have tried the following commands (on HPC) to download both data:
/usr/local/software/wbic/bin/dcmconv.pl -remoteae P00592 -id 22455 -date 20230727 -info -makedir -outtype dicom
/usr/local/software/wbic/bin/dcmconv.pl -remoteae P00461 -id 34871 -date 20230727 -info -makedir -outtype dicom
Unfortunately, the response we received was "No Images" and we haven't been able to retrieve the data successfully.
Is there any issue with the cluster? What would you suggest?
Best regards,
Onur
PS: I can access the old data by running this command:
/usr/local/software/wbic/bin/dcmconv.pl remoteae P00418 -ID 28069 -date 20230707 -info -makedir -outtype dicom
I guess there is some issue with the recent data.
============================================
Onur Ozyurt, Ph.D.
Research Associate, University of Cambridge
Wolfson Brain Imaging Centre (WBIC)
Department of Clinical Neurosciences
Addenbrooke's Hospital, Biomedical Campus
Hills Road, Cambridge, CB2 0QQ, UK
============================================

[TICKET_SUMMARY] MRI data in Dicom format

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear HPC support team,
I am trying to download MRI data  in dicom format using the command dcmconv.pl -remoteae P00550 -outttype dicom. But the data is downloaded in nifti format and not dicom. I'm not sure what I'm doing wrong here. Can you please help me with this?
Thanks!
Livia 
Livia Tomova, PhD
Henslow Research Fellow
Hughes Hall
University of Cambridge
[http://tomova.scripts.mit.edu/tomova](http://tomova.scripts.mit.edu/tomova)

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Downloading imaging data from XNAT databridge

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear HPC support,
I'm trying to download imaging data that has been collected at 
Addenbrokes' MRIS service and then transferred to the XNAT repository 
through a data bridge that Guy Williams sat up in place. I can access it 
with my laptop using:
[https://xnat01.wbic.cam.ac.uk/app/template/Login.vm#](https://xnat01.wbic.cam.ac.uk/app/template/Login.vm#)!
However, it seems that there is a permission issue when I tried to 
download it directly from the CSD3 (see screenshot). I tried downloading 
to my laptop and then transfer it to the server via WinSCP but it is 
extremely inefficient. Could you please guide me on how to download this 
data?
Thank you in advance,
Rafa.
– 
Rafael Romero García, PhD
Cancer Research UK
Wolfson Research Fellow
Department of Psychiatry
University of Cambridge
[http://www.bcni.psychol.cam.ac.uk/directory/romero-garcia](http://www.bcni.psychol.cam.ac.uk/directory/romero-garcia)

I was wondering if you might be able to help with this issue?

Joe

user_input:
Dear HPC team,
I am Tigran, an MPhil student in ACS. I am using the CSD3 server for my MPhil dissertation, the slurm jobs that I submit do not leave the queue and start executing.
When I run mybalance command, it shows that the available hours is 1, is this the cause?
Best,
Tigran
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / Account Balance / Project Credits
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (jobs not starting)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports that submitted Slurm jobs remain in the queue and notes that `mybalance` shows only 1 computing hour remaining.

If a project account has insufficient remaining compute hours, jobs charged to that account will not start.

ACTION

1) Confirm cause
   - If `mybalance` shows only 1 remaining compute hour on the specified project account, this is likely preventing the job from running.

2) Advise corrective step
   - Cancel the queued job.
   - Resubmit using a project account with sufficient available hours (e.g. an SL3-GPU account if appropriate and available to the user).

3) Do NOT:
   - Manually add hours.
   - Override scheduler/accounting restrictions.
   - Escalate unless the balance appears incorrect.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Tigran,

Yes, if `mybalance` shows that there is only 1 computing hour remaining on the LIO-SL2-GPU account, this would prevent your job from starting.

Please cancel the current job and resubmit it using a project account with sufficient available hours.

Best regards,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply accounting errors without verification.
- Do not bypass project credit limits.
- Ensure the user selects an account they are authorised to use.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Job Management / Queue Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user's jobs are not running, and the `mybalance` command shows only 1 hour available. This could be due to insufficient CPU hours or other issues with the job submission script. Similar issues have been reported in previous tickets where jobs were stuck due to insufficient hours or other reasons [1][2][3][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the user's available CPU hours using the `mybalance` command.
2. Check the job queue status using the `squeue` command to identify any pending reasons.
3. If the issue is due to insufficient hours, advise the user on purchasing more hours or waiting for the next quarter's allocation.
4. If the issue persists, escalate to the HPC administrator for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the output of the `squeue -u <your_username>` command?
- Have you recently purchased additional CPU hours or are you waiting for the next quarter's allocation?
- Can you share the SLURM script you are using to submit your jobs?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Tigran,

Thank you for your message. The issue with your jobs not running could be due to insufficient CPU hours or other reasons. Please provide the output of the `squeue -u <your_username>` command so we can check the job queue status. Additionally, if you have recently purchased additional CPU hours or are waiting for the next quarter's allocation, please let us know.

If the issue persists, we may need to escalate this to the HPC administrator for further investigation.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Always verify the user's available CPU hours before advising on purchasing more hours.
- Escalate to the HPC administrator if the issue cannot be resolved through standard troubleshooting steps.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-15240::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-14249::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-20099::chunk::0004
[4] : HPCSSUP-16481::chunk::0000

<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Amanah,
No, I’m afraid SL2 is the highest priority in the cluster. We know it has been quite busy lately which leads to longer wait times but we are working on adding more capacity which would make job running quicker.
Kind regards,
Avraam

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0002 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Dear Avraam,
My cpu jobs have been queuing for a week now. I don't have access to any other resources i can use for my work.
BW,
Amanah

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0003 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Amanah,
All your jobs are pending with reason “AssocGrpCPUMinutesLimit”. You can find this by running the command “squeue -u asl58”. This reason means that your slurm project has run out hours so your jobs can’t run. You can find the amount of CPU/GPU hours available for your projects with the command “mybalance”. If you are using the SL2 project you can purchase more hours. If you are using SL3 the free hours will be replenished the next quarter.
Kind regards,
Avraam

</MESSAGE>

Dear Amanah,
No, I’m afraid SL2 is the highest priority in the cluster. We know it has been quite busy lately which leads to longer wait times but we are working on adding more capacity which would make job running quicker.
Kind regards,
Avraam

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0002 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Dear Avraam,
My cpu jobs have been queuing for a week now. I don't have access to any other resources i can use for my work.
BW,
Amanah

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0003 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Amanah,
All your jobs are pending with reason “AssocGrpCPUMinutesLimit”. You can find this by running the command “squeue -u asl58”. This reason means that your slurm project has run out hours so your jobs can’t run. You can find the amount of CPU/GPU hours available for your projects with the command “mybalance”. If you are using the SL2 project you can purchase more hours. If you are using SL3 the free hours will be replenished the next quarter.
Kind regards,
Avraam

</MESSAGE>

user_input:
Hello,
I’ve been directed from the UIS support team. I am writing to request a quote for 200 SL2 GPU hours and for the creation of an SL2 GPU account under Dr. Tian Zhao’s name (crsid: txz20). Our current GPU account is ZHAO-SL3-GPU.
Could you please let me know what information is required from our end? I would also appreciate it if you could outline the process for purchasing these resources and for account creation, including any approvals that may be needed. This will allow us to proceed with raising a purchase order.
Thank you very much for your help.
Kind Regards,
Fiona Charlier
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Procurement / GPU Resource Purchase / Quotation
Technical Action Required: No (user to follow published process)
Finance Action Required: Yes (user departmental PO required)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requests:
- A quote for 200 SL2-GPU hours
- Creation of an SL2-GPU account under a named PI

GPU hours and account creation follow the published charges and procurement process. Pricing and ordering instructions are provided on the official charges page.

ACTION

1) Direct user to official pricing page:
   https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/charges

2) Advise user to:
   - Review the published SL2-GPU pricing.
   - Follow the process for raising a purchase order via their departmental finance team.
   - Submit required account creation details as described on the charges page.

3) Do NOT:
   - Generate ad hoc pricing outside published rates.
   - Create accounts before procurement process is followed.
   - Escalate unless pricing discrepancy or policy exception is requested.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Fiona,

Please follow the instructions and pricing information available on our charges page:

https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/charges

This page outlines the process for purchasing SL2-GPU hours and for requesting account creation. Once your departmental finance team has raised the appropriate purchase order, we can proceed accordingly.

Best regards,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not provide unofficial pricing.
- Do not create accounts without the required procurement steps.
- Ensure users follow the documented purchase workflow.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Resource Purchase / Account Creation
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The process for purchasing SL2 GPU hours and creating an account involves raising a purchase order (PO) and sending it to the HPC team. The PO should include the number of hours, the type of resource (GPU), and the name of the account or PI. The current cost for SL2 GPU hours is £0.55 per hour [1][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Confirm the details provided by the user:
   - Item: SL2 GPU hours
   - Quantity: 200
   - PI name: Dr. Tian Zhao (crsid: txz20)
   - Account name: ZHAO-SL2-GPU
2. Send an informal email quote to the user with the cost details (200 hours * £0.55 per hour = £110) [1].
3. Inform the user to raise a PO to Information Services for the desired number and type of resource units, mentioning the name of the project or PI owning the new resources, and email the PDF of the PO to purchases@hpc.cam.ac.uk [1].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Fiona,

Thank you for your request. Here are the steps to proceed with purchasing 200 SL2 GPU hours and creating an account under Dr. Tian Zhao’s name:

1. We will send you an informal email quote for 200 SL2 GPU hours. The current cost is £0.55 per hour, so the total cost will be £110.
2. Please raise a purchase order (PO) to Information Services for the desired number and type of resource units. Make sure to mention the name of the project or PI owning the new resources (Dr. Tian Zhao, crsid: txz20) and the account name (ZHAO-SL2-GPU).
3. Email the PDF of the PO to purchases@hpc.cam.ac.uk.

Please let us know if you have any further questions.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure the PO includes all necessary details such as the number of hours, type of resource, and the name of the account or PI [1][4][5].
- Do not process the request without a valid PO [1][4][5].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-83224::chunk::0001
[4] : HPCSSUP-43877::chunk::0001
[5] : HPCSSUP-38829::chunk::0006

Dear CSD3 support team,
I am able to log in to login.hpc.cam.ac.uk with my username and password, after which I am prompted to enter a TOTP code.
However, I have not yet been able to set up CSD3 MFA:
- I never received a QR code for CSD3 TOTP
- login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk reports invalid username or password
- connecting to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk closes the connection immediately after password entry
Could you please help me with this? Thank you in advance!
My username: jh2642
Best regards,
Jin Huang
—
Jin Huang
Marie Skłodowska-Curie Postdoctoral Fellow
@ AFAR Lab<[https://cambridge-afar.github.io/](https://cambridge-afar.github.io/)>, University of Cambridge
[https://www.cst.cam.ac.uk/people/jh2642](https://www.cst.cam.ac.uk/people/jh2642)
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / Account Provisioning / MFA
Technical Action Required: No (account application required)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (user cannot complete login)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports:
- Able to reach login.hpc.cam.ac.uk
- Prompted for TOTP
- Has not received QR code
- login-web reports invalid username/password
- multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk disconnects after password

This strongly indicates that the user does not yet have a fully provisioned CSD3 account with MFA enrolment enabled.

MFA (TOTP) enrolment occurs only after an account has been formally created via the official HPC application process.

ACTION

1) Confirm whether the user has applied for an HPC account
   Direct them to:
   https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/rcs-application

2) Explain that:
   - Account creation must be triggered via the official application form.
   - MFA enrolment (QR code) is generated only after the account is provisioned.
   - The behaviour they are seeing is consistent with an account not yet fully created.

3) Do NOT:
   - Reset MFA (no MFA exists yet).
   - Attempt manual account creation via ticket.
   - Troubleshoot login-web further until account status is confirmed.

4) If the user confirms they have already applied:
   - Check provisioning status internally.
   - Escalate only if application is stuck in provisioning.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: SSH login requests TOTP but MFA not set up yet

Hello Jin,

Have you previously applied for an account via the official HPC application form?

https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/rcs-application

If not, please complete this form with your details and your PI’s details and submit it. This will trigger the account provisioning process. Once your account is created, you will then be able to enrol in MFA (you will be presented with a QR code during login).

If you have already submitted the form, please let us know and we can check the status for you.

Best regards,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not assume MFA malfunction when account may not yet exist.
- Do not attempt to bypass the official account creation workflow.
- MFA enrolment is tied to successful account provisioning.

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear CSD3 Support Team,
I am an internal Cambridge user and am trying to log in to CSD3 for the first time.
According to the onboarding instructions, I should first SSH to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk to generate a TOTP MFA secret. However, when I attempt to SSH to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk using my CRSid and UIS password, the connection is closed immediately after authentication.
As a result, I am unable to generate a TOTP secret or complete MFA setup, and I am also unable to log in to the CSD3 login nodes.

*

Millie

Dear Support,
I'm William Lee, working with Mike Roberts e.g. MROBERTS-SL3-GPU and AUTOQC-DAMTP-SL2-GPU.
I'd like to run TensorFlow on ampere and I've been trying to follow the instructions here:
[https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/tensorflow.html](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/tensorflow.html)
But the line
module load python/3.8.11/gcc-9.4.0-yb6rzr6
doesn't work.
Is there an updated version of the instructions or a drop-in replacement for this line please?
Best wishes,
William.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / TensorFlow / Ampere GPU / Modules
Technical Action Required: No (user environment clarification)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is attempting to follow the official TensorFlow GPU instructions:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/tensorflow.html

They report that:

  module load python/3.8.11/gcc-9.4.0-yb6rzr6

“doesn’t work”.

The documentation indicates that these steps must be run within an interactive session on an Ampere GPU node. The Python module is available within the Ampere software stack. If the module cannot be loaded, this is most likely due to the environment context (e.g. not being on an Ampere node or the module environment having been altered).

There is no evidence that the documentation is outdated or that the module has been removed.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm execution context

Ask the user to confirm they are running the instructions inside an interactive session on an Ampere GPU node.

Example:

  srun --partition=ampere --gres=gpu:1 --time=01:00:00 --pty bash

Then check module availability:

  module avail python/3.8.11

Expected output should include:

  python/3.8.11/gcc-9.4.0-yb6rzr6

2) Attempt module load

If the module appears in module avail, attempt:

  module load python/3.8.11/gcc-9.4.0-yb6rzr6

3) If the module still does not load

Ask the user to provide the exact error message produced when running:

  module load python/3.8.11/gcc-9.4.0-yb6rzr6

and confirm:
  - The hostname (to verify they are on an Ampere GPU node)
  - Whether they have modified the module environment (e.g. using module purge)

Only escalate if:
  - The module does not appear in module avail while on an Ampere node, or
  - The load error indicates a system-side module configuration issue.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello William,

The TensorFlow instructions must be run within an interactive session on an Ampere GPU node.

Could you please start an Ampere session, for example:

  srun --partition=ampere --gres=gpu:1 --time=01:00:00 --pty bash

and then run:

  module avail python/3.8.11

You should see:

  python/3.8.11/gcc-9.4.0-yb6rzr6

If it appears but does not load, please send us the exact error message and the output of:

  hostname

so we can investigate further.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not assume the module has been removed without checking module avail.
- Do not recommend installing a custom Python or modifying system module paths.
- Keep troubleshooting limited to confirming correct execution context and module visibility.

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Tensorflow Help

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear IT Support,
I am Theo Lewy (CRSID tal43) and my PI is Rich Kerswell (project KERSWELL-SL3).
I am currently trying to get tensorflow working on the ampere GPUs but I am having trouble with this. The documentation only talks about rhel7, and I am getting conflicts when I follow the commands in “Running Tensorflow with GPU acceleration on CSD3” and pip install dependencies ([https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/tensorflow.html](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/tensorflow.html)) so I have instead been trying to set it up using rhel8.
I have currently tried using the attached .sh script. The attached slurm script is my output. The error is (I think) due to the fact that all of the modules I have loaded use cuda-11.4, while tensorflow-2.11.0 is built to be compatible with cuda-11.2.
I cannot unload cuda-11.4 without getting the message “ERROR: cuda/11.4 cannot be unloaded due to a prereq", and so I can’t work out what set of modules should by loaded so that I can run tensorflow on the ampere GPU nodes. Is there a standard set of modules that should be loaded, and a standard shell script that would let me run tensorflow?
Best wishes,
Theo

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Running tensorflow on Ampere nodes

[CONTEXT]
@Kacper Kornet I think you may be able to advise this user? Thank you!

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0004 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Hi Henry,
Just to be clear - that documentation you shared doesn't work this was the issue I raised in my first message.
""
Running:
module purge
module load rhel7/default-gpu
module unload cuda/8.0
module load python/3.6 cuda/10.0 cudnn/7.5_cuda-10.0
As described at:
[https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/tensorflow.htm](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/tensorflow.htm)
Returns:
protobuf requires Python '>=3.7' but the running Python is 3.6.8
""
Thanks a lot,
Sam
–
Samuel AK Leeney
Physics PhD candidate
Teaching Associate, Queens' College
Cavendish Astrophysics Group
University of Cambridge
cavendishradiocosmology.com
github.com/samleeney

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0005 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Samuel ,
From the quoted error message
> protobuf requires Python '>=3.7' but the running Python is 3.6.8
The system tells us that we need a version of python newer than 3.6.8 (in this instance Python 3.7 is needed). This means that you will have to install your own virtual environment (e.g., Conda) and configure from there. Instructions may be found at
> Instructions for this may be found here: https://docs.anaconda.com/miniconda/#miniconda-latest-installer-links 
> You can use `wget` to download the `Linux x86_64 installer`
> Then you will need to `chmod +x` and run the script. Thanks
Best wishes, 
Henry
HPC Support

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0006 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Hi Henry,
I was following the instructions in the cambridge hpc documentation directly, and while already in a Python virtual environment, so I don't think this is a solution.
I believe the problem is that your documentation is out of date (as it uses rhel7, not rhel8). My colleagues are also unable to use tensorflow using the cambridge hpc docs.
Do you have any other suggestions?
Best,
Sam
–
Samuel AK Leeney
Physics PhD candidate
Teaching Associate, Queens' College
Cavendish Astrophysics Group
University of Cambridge
cavendishradiocosmology.com
github.com/samleeney

</MESSAGE>

I cannot unload cuda-11.4 without getting the message “ERROR: cuda/11.4 cannot be unloaded due to a prereq", and so I can’t work out what set of modules should by loaded so that I can run tensorflow on the ampere GPU nodes. Is there a standard set of modules that should be loaded, and a standard shell script that would let me run tensorflow?
Best wishes,
Theo

The system tells us that we need a version of python newer than 3.6.8 (in this instance Python 3.7 is needed). This means that you will have to install your own virtual environment (e.g., Conda) and configure from there. Instructions may be found at
> Instructions for this may be found here: https://docs.anaconda.com/miniconda/#miniconda-latest-installer-links 
> You can use `wget` to download the `Linux x86_64 installer`

```
module purge
```

user_input:
Hello,
For some reason I cannot log into the CSD3 headnodes. I was logged in yesterday, and after some idle period I had to log back in, and since then I cannot log in.
I enter my password and MFA details, then it requests for password again (seemingly looking like my password is wrong, though I'm sure I've entered it correctly as I had been logging in and out lately).
Do you have any ideas? (user vm462)
Cheers,
Vijay
Dr Vijay Mahatma
Research Associate
Battcock Centre for Experimental Astrophysics (Room G04)
Cavendish Laboratory, University of Cambridge
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / SSH Login / Account Lockout
Technical Action Required: No (diagnosis + user guidance)
Escalation Required: No (unless lock persists beyond normal timeout)
Urgency: Medium (user access blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports:
- Previously able to log into CSD3 headnodes.
- After an idle period, login fails.
- After entering password and MFA, system prompts for password again.
- Appears as though password is incorrect.

This pattern is consistent with an account lock due to multiple unsuccessful login attempts. On CSD3, repeated failed authentication attempts can trigger a temporary lock, which then clears automatically after a timeout period.

A possible contributing factor is that the user may be entering an outdated UIS/Raven password; if they recently changed it, the new one should be used on CSD3 as well.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Check account status (internal)
   - Confirm whether the account is in a locked state due to failed attempts.
   - Confirm there is no wider authentication incident.

2) Inform user of temporary lock behaviour
   - Explain that repeated failed login attempts can trigger a temporary lock.
   - State that the lock clears automatically after approximately 30 minutes.
   - No manual reset is required unless the lock persists.

3) Clarify correct credential usage
   - User must enter their current UIS/Raven password (not an old HPC-specific password).
   - After password, they must provide the correct TOTP associated with CSD3 (e.g. SSH token).

4) If issue persists after timeout
   Request:
     - Exact hostname used (e.g. login.hpc.cam.ac.uk, login-icelake, multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk)
     - Exact error message shown after failure
     - Confirmation that UIS/Raven password works for other services

5) Escalate only if:
   - Lock does not clear after timeout window
   - Account shows unexpected authentication failure state
   - MFA system appears inconsistent

DO NOT:
- Reset the password.
- Reset MFA without evidence.
- Suggest creating a new account.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: CSD3 login issue

Dear Vijay,

Your symptoms are consistent with a temporary account lock caused by multiple unsuccessful login attempts. If that is the case, the lock should clear automatically after approximately 30 minutes.

Once it has cleared, please ensure that you are entering your current UIS/Raven password when prompted in the terminal, followed by your CSD3 MFA code.

If you continue to experience issues after waiting for the lock to clear, please let us know the exact hostname you are connecting to and the full error message shown.

Best wishes,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not disclose internal authentication logs.
- Do not confirm password correctness.
- Avoid manual intervention unless lock persists abnormally.
- UIS/Raven password changes automatically propagate to CSD3 authentication.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / MFA Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing a login loop where they are repeatedly prompted for their password and MFA details. This issue has been reported by multiple users and is often related to incorrect MFA token usage or account lockout due to multiple failed login attempts [1][2][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify that the user is using the correct MFA token called "CSD3: SSH" [2][4].
2. Check if the user's account is locked due to multiple failed login attempts [3].
3. If the issue persists, escalate to the technical team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm that you are using the MFA token called "CSD3: SSH"?
- Have you tried resetting your password?
- Have you recently changed your university password?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Vijay,

Thank you for your message. It seems like you are experiencing a login loop, which is a known issue often related to MFA token usage or account lockout.

Please ensure that you are using the MFA token called "CSD3: SSH" [2][4]. If you have tried logging in multiple times unsuccessfully, your account might be locked. In this case, please let us know so we can assist you further.

If the issue persists, we may need to escalate this to our technical team for further investigation.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Always verify the correct MFA token usage before proceeding with other troubleshooting steps [2][4].
- Be cautious when handling account lockout issues to prevent unauthorized access attempts [3].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-89787::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-84076::chunk::0001
[3] : HPCSSUP-97002::chunk::0000
[4] : HPCSSUP-69965::chunk::0001

user_input:
Dear Team,
I appreciate your help with setting up a level 3 (given high IP value) SRCP platform for TumourVue - cancer surgery trials data. Three of my masters students will have access. The data sharing agreement is in place.
The students will start next week and I would be grateful if I could please have your support as a high priority.
Thanks a lot
Gita
—
Gita Khalili Moghaddam, PhD
Principal Investigator
Department of Clinical Neurosciences
University of Cambridge
CB2 0QQ
Royal Society Industry Fellow
GSK Global Health
While it suits me to email outside normal working hours, I do not expect a response outside your own.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SRCP / New Platform Provisioning / Resource Request
Technical Action Required: Yes (new SRCP platform setup)
Portal/Form Action Required: Yes (SRCP resource request form submission)
Escalation Required: Yes (SRCP platform provisioning team)
Urgency: Medium–High (students starting next week; high IP / Level 3 data)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The PI is requesting the creation of a new SRCP Level 3 platform (high IP value) for a new project (TumourVue), separate from an existing platform (Predict), with funding in place for one year. Three Masters students will require access with permissions similar to an existing workflow (Python-based image analysis).

This is not a simple user-access request. It requires submission of an SRCP resource request form to initiate provisioning of a new platform, including:
- Platform creation
- Security level confirmation (Level 3)
- Funding confirmation
- Storage and compute allocation
- Role definitions

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Clarify request type
   - Confirm that this is a new SRCP platform (not just additional users).
   - Confirm that Level 3 classification is required.

2) Direct PI to SRCP resource request form
   Provide link:
     https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/form/srcp-resource-request

   Explain that this form initiates:
     - New platform provisioning
     - Security review
     - Resource allocation
     - Funding linkage

3) Provide documentation for security levels
   Refer to:
     https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/srcp/user-onboarding/index.html#user-onboarding

   Clarify:
     - Level 3 environments are designed for high IP / sensitive research data.
     - Appropriate role selection must be specified for student users.

4) After submission (internal)
   - Confirm form submission received.
   - Attach updates to the resource request ticket.
   - Escalate to SRCP provisioning team.
   - Do not promise specific timelines until confirmed.

5) Do NOT:
   - Manually create the platform outside the formal workflow.
   - Add users before platform provisioning is complete.
   - Confirm approval until security and provisioning checks are complete.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NOT CLEAR IN FORM)
- Confirm expected duration (1 year initially).
- Confirm estimated storage requirements.
- Confirm number of users and roles required.
- Confirm whether this is fully isolated from existing Predict platform.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: TumourVue SRCP Platform Request

Dear Gita,

To create a new SRCP platform for TumourVue, you will need to submit an SRCP resource request using the following form:

  https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/form/srcp-resource-request

This will initiate the provisioning process for a new Level 3 platform, including security review and resource allocation.

You may also find the following documentation helpful when selecting the appropriate level and roles:

  https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/srcp/user-onboarding/index.html#user-onboarding

Once the form has been submitted, we will process the request and provide updates via the associated ticket.

Best wishes,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not bypass the SRCP resource request process.
- Ensure Level 3 designation is formally captured in the request.
- Do not grant access prior to completion of provisioning and security approval.
- Avoid committing to deadlines until the provisioning team confirms scheduling.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SRCP / Platform Setup
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Yes
Urgency: High

QUICK ASSESSMENT
Gita Khalili Moghaddam has requested the setup of a level 3 SRCP platform for the TumourVue project, which involves cancer surgery trials data. The data sharing agreement is in place, and three master's students will have access. The request is urgent as the students will start next week.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Escalate the ticket to the relevant SRCP team for immediate attention.
2. Confirm with Gita the specific details of the data sharing agreement and any additional requirements for the platform setup.
3. Ensure that the platform is set up with the necessary security measures for level 3 data.
4. Provide Gita with the steps to onboard her students to the SRCP platform once it is set up.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Gita,

Thank you for your request. We have escalated your ticket to the relevant SRCP team for immediate attention. We will need to confirm the specific details of the data sharing agreement and any additional requirements for the platform setup.

Please provide us with the following information:
- The specific details of the data sharing agreement.
- Any additional requirements for the platform setup.

We will ensure that the platform is set up with the necessary security measures for level 3 data and provide you with the steps to onboard your students to the SRCP platform once it is set up.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that all data compliance requirements are met before granting access to the platform.
- Follow the established procedures for setting up SRCP platforms and onboarding users.

REFERENCE KEY

Escalation Required: No

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User attempted to create a conda environment with Python 3.12 and NumPy 2.3.5 and hit an `UnsatisfiableError`. From the visible commands, this is most likely a dependency-resolution conflict rather than a CSD3-specific failure. The safest guidance is to avoid forcing incompatible version pins, start from a clean user-managed environment, and inspect any additional requirements file if one is involved.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Recommend a clean user-managed Conda install if not already using one
   - Install Miniconda in `hpc-work` rather than relying on an outdated shared install.

2) Create a fresh environment with an explicit Python version, e.g.:
   - `conda create -n myenv python=3.12 -y`
   - `conda activate myenv`

3) Avoid forcing strict NumPy 2.x pins until compatibility is confirmed
   - Let Conda resolve NumPy automatically, or inspect any requirements file for packages that still expect NumPy 1.x.

4) If a `requirements.txt` is involved, review it for conflicting hard version pins before installing the full stack.

5) If the problem persists, ask for:
   - the full `requirements.txt`
   - the exact Conda commands used
   - output of `conda info` and `conda list`

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Subject: Re: Conda environment conflicts on CSD3

Hi Nico,

The error you’re seeing looks like a dependency-resolution conflict rather than a problem specific to CSD3.

We recommend creating a clean user-managed Conda environment and avoiding strict NumPy 2.x pins until the rest of the package set has been checked for compatibility. If you are using a `requirements.txt`, please review that file for conflicting version pins as well.

If the issue continues, please send us the full `requirements.txt`, the exact commands you used, and the outputs of `conda info` and `conda list`.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not recommend modifying system Python.
- Do not suggest installing packages into central/shared environments.
- Avoid advising installation on compute nodes.
- Keep all installs within user-controlled directories (e.g. hpc-work).

1. Create the environment in your /hpc-work area using the `--prefix` option. For example:
   ```
   conda create --prefix /rds/user/<your_username>/hpc-work/env_name python=3.12
   ```

3. Try installing the packages one by one to identify specific conflicts.

If the issue persists, please let us know, and we will escalate it for further investigation.

 miniconda and then using the following:

user_input:
Hi Sir/Madam,
Hope you are doing well! I am having some troubles in SSHing into the HPC.
I can successfully log into [https://login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk](https://login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk/) using my CRSid (vd327), and my HPC account appears to be active.
However, when I try to log in via SSH (e.g. ssh vd327@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk), I am prompted for my password and then a TOTP verification code, but after three attempts I receive “Permission denied”.
I have verified that:
- I am using my Cambridge Raven/UIS password
- MFA/TOTP is set up and working for login-web
- The SSH host key has been accepted correctly
Could you please advise me on what I should do/ how to fix this?
Many thanks,
Vivek Dherani
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / SSH / MFA (TOTP)
Technical Action Required: No (user configuration issue)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (access blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User can successfully log into login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk but receives “Permission denied” when attempting SSH login to login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk.

User reports:
- Raven/UIS password is correct.
- MFA works for login-web.
- SSH host key accepted.
- Password recently changed.

Web login MFA and SSH MFA are separate enrolments. A common cause of this behaviour is that MFA has not yet been enrolled for SSH access (multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk), even though web MFA works.

ACTION

1) Explain SSH MFA requirement
   - Clarify that SSH requires a separate MFA enrolment from login-web.

2) Provide documentation
   https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/mfa.html#walkthrough-ssh-to-multi-hpc-cam-ac-uk

3) Instruct user to enrol SSH MFA via:

   ssh <CRSid>@multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk

   They should:
   - Enter UIS/Raven password.
   - Scan the displayed QR code into their authenticator app.
   - Confirm a new token is created (label similar to “CSD3:SSH Login”).

4) After enrolment, retry SSH to:

   ssh <CRSid>@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk

5) If this does not resolve the issue:
   - Ask the user to paste the full SSH output (including prompts but excluding any codes).
   - Escalate only if MFA enrolment succeeds but authentication still fails.

EXPECTED OUTCOME
Once SSH MFA is enrolled correctly, authentication via SSH should succeed using:
- UIS/Raven password
- TOTP from the new “CSD3:SSH Login” token

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Vivek,

Although your MFA is working for login-web, SSH access requires MFA to be set up separately.

A common cause of this behaviour is that SSH MFA has not yet been enrolled. Please follow the walkthrough here:

https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/mfa.html#walkthrough-ssh-to-multi-hpc-cam-ac-uk

You can begin the enrolment process by running:

  ssh <CRSid>@multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk

You will be prompted for your UIS/Raven password and then shown a QR code to scan into your authenticator app.

Once this is complete, please try SSH again to:

  ssh <CRSid>@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk

If the issue persists, please paste the full SSH output and we’ll investigate further.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not assume password error if login-web works.
- Do not reset MFA without identity verification.
- Do not suggest bypassing MFA.
- Avoid implying internal verification of MFA enrolment unless explicitly confirmed.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Access / SSH Login
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing a "Permission denied" error after entering their password and TOTP verification code when attempting to SSH into the HPC. This issue is commonly reported and may be related to password or TOTP configuration [1][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the user's account status and ensure that the password and TOTP settings are correctly configured.
2. Check if there are any recent changes to the user's account or network settings that could affect SSH access.
3. If the issue persists, escalate to the technical team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Have you recently changed your Raven/UIS password?
- Have you tried resetting your TOTP configuration in the Microsoft Authenticator app?
- Are you able to log in to other University of Cambridge services using the same credentials?

user_input:
Hi,
I can’t submit any Slurm jobs using the Callender+ account, PI Dr Callender copied in,  because it doesn’t seem to have any partitions attached to it, so every sbatch call fails with an “Invalid account or account/partition combination” error. I can install packages on the login node, but can’t see that my environment works end-to-end without running jobs on compute nodes. I would be grateful for your advice on whether a CPU partition is available under callender+ and how to resolve this please?
Best wishes,
Megha
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / Account & Partition Configuration
Technical Action Required: No (user-side correction)
Escalation Required: No (unless account not present in mybalance)
Urgency: Medium (job submission blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports:

  sbatch: error: Batch job submission failed:
  Invalid account or account/partition combination specified

They attempted to submit using:
  #SBATCH --account=callender+
  #SBATCH --partition=cclake

This error typically indicates one of the following:

1) The Slurm account name is incorrect (account names are case-sensitive).
2) The account name does not exactly match the allocation shown in Slurm.
3) The account/partition combination is not valid for the user.
4) The user is not using the correct Slurm account identifier format.

The correct way to determine valid accounts is via the `mybalance` command.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Ask the user to confirm available accounts
   Instruct them to run:

     mybalance

   This will display the exact account names and usage limits.

2) Confirm account string matches exactly
   - Account names are case-sensitive.
   - Hyphens and underscores are distinct.
   - Plus signs (“+”) are not normally used in Slurm account names.

3) Ensure correct SBATCH syntax
   In the submission script:

     #SBATCH --account=<EXACT_ACCOUNT_NAME_FROM_MYBALANCE>
     #SBATCH --partition=<VALID_PARTITION_FOR_ACCOUNT>

4) Validate partition compatibility
   If unsure, ask the user to provide:

     mybalance
     sinfo -s

   Confirm that:
   - The account is listed.
   - The partition is valid for that account.
   - The user is a member of that Slurm account.

5) If the account does not appear in `mybalance`
   - Verify account membership.
   - Escalate to cluster administration if necessary.

COMMON CAUSES TO CHECK

- Typographical error in account name.
- Using an informal label (e.g. project nickname) instead of the Slurm account identifier.
- Case mismatch.
- Replacing hyphens with underscores.
- Using outdated account name.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Unable to submit Slurm jobs on callender+

Hello Megha,

The error you’re seeing usually indicates that the account name or account/partition combination is not valid.

Could you please run:

  mybalance

This will show the exact Slurm account names available to you. Account names are case-sensitive and must match exactly in your submission script.

Once you have confirmed the correct account name, update your script to include:

  #SBATCH --account=<exact account name>
  #SBATCH --partition=<valid partition>

If you’re still seeing the error after confirming the account name from `mybalance`, please send the output of `mybalance` and your updated submission script so we can take a closer look.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not assume account misconfiguration without verifying `mybalance`.
- Do not manually alter Slurm accounts unless membership is confirmed missing.
- Avoid guessing account names; always use system-reported identifiers.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Job Submission / Account Configuration
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is unable to submit Slurm jobs due to an "Invalid account or account/partition combination" error. This issue is commonly related to missing or incorrect partition access for the specified account [1][2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the user's account and partition access.
2. Check if the account "callender+" has the necessary partitions attached.
3. If the partitions are missing, add the user to the appropriate partitions (e.g., cclake, icelake, skylake, and their himem variants) [1][2][3][4][5].
4. Confirm the user's access and test job submission.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact Slurm job submission script you are using?
- Have you checked your account balance and confirmed that you have access to the "callender+" account?
- What specific partitions are you trying to submit jobs to?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Megha,

Thank you for your message. The error you are encountering is typically due to missing or incorrect partition access for your account. We will verify your account and partition access and ensure that the "callender+" account has the necessary partitions attached.

Please provide the exact Slurm job submission script you are using, and confirm that you have checked your account balance and have access to the "callender+" account. Additionally, let us know which specific partitions you are trying to submit jobs to.

We will get back to you as soon as we have more information.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that partition access is granted only after verifying the user's account and the PI's approval.
- Do not grant access to partitions without proper authorization.

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
To whom it may concern,  
I am Dongcheng Jiang and my HPC username (CRSid) is dj346. I tried to follow the guidance on submitting array jobs ([https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/batch.html#array-jobs](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/batch.html#array-jobs)). Using the command mybalance, I can see 4 accounts: WOODLAND-SL2-CPU, WOODLAND-SL3-CPU, WOODLAND-SL2-GPU, WOODLAND-SL3-GPU, and I believe I should be able to submit SL3 CPU jobs.  
This is my submission script: /rds/user/dj346/hpc-work/espnet/egs/an4/asr1/submit_array_test  
When I tried to submit the jobs via the command sbatch submit_array_test (I was already under the directory /rds/user/dj346/hpc-work/espnet/egs/an4/asr1/), the following error was reported: 
 
sbatch: error: Batch job submission failed: Invalid account or account/partition combination specified  
I also investigated the problem by trying to start interactive SL3 CPU sessions using this command: 
sintr -A WOODLAND-SL3-CPU -p cclake -N1 -n1 -t 1:0:0 --qos=INTR
, and the same error message occurred. I tried cclake, icelake, skylake for -p option, but the same error occurred repeatedly. 
 
So could you please help me find out what was going wrong with my SL3 CPU submission? Thank you very much and I am looking forward to your reply. 
 
Best wishes, 
Dongcheng

Hello HPC support team,
We'd like to add Cambridge MPhil students Boqiao Zhang (bz317) and Oszkar Urban (ou222) to:
1) RDS disk
`rds-posecraft-5SVx1x5hoTY`
2) My supervisor Prof Cengiz Oztireli's accounts
OZTIRELI-SL2-CPU
OZTIRELI-SL2-GPU
OZTIRELI-SL3-CPU
OZTIRELI-SL3-GPU
Please let us know if anything or any approval is needed. Many thanks!
Best wishes,
Zhilin
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage Membership / Slurm Account Access
Technical Action Required: Yes (conditional – Slurm account membership)
Portal Action Required: Yes (RDS membership via Self Service)
Escalation Required: Conditional (PI approval required for Slurm accounts)
Urgency: Low–Medium (collaborator onboarding)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requests:

1) Add two students to an RDS storage project:
   rds-posecraft-5SVx1x5hoTY

2) Add the same users to multiple Slurm accounts:
   OZTIRELI-SL2-CPU
   OZTIRELI-SL2-GPU
   OZTIRELI-SL3-CPU
   OZTIRELI-SL3-GPU

Key principles:

- RDS project membership is managed by the Data Owner or Data Manager via the Self Service portal. HPC Support does not directly administer storage memberships.
- Slurm account access can only be granted with explicit approval from the PI/account owner.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) RDS Membership
   - Inform user that storage project membership is controlled via the Self Service portal.
   - Direct them to confirm that the Data Owner or Data Manager has added the students.
   - If necessary, advise them to check:
       https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/

2) Slurm Account Access
   - Confirm whether explicit approval from the account owner (PI) has been provided.
   - If not, request written approval from the PI before proceeding.
   - Once approval is received, add the users to the specified Slurm accounts.

3) Do NOT:
   - Add users to Slurm accounts without PI approval.
   - Modify storage memberships manually.
   - Assume requester has authority unless they are confirmed Data Owner/PI.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (IF APPROVAL NOT CLEAR)

- Are you the Data Owner or Data Manager of the RDS project?
- Can the PI/account owner confirm approval for adding these users to the Slurm accounts?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Adding collaborators to RDS and Slurm accounts

Dear Zhilin,

Regarding the RDS project, storage memberships are managed by the Data Owner or Data Manager via the Self Service portal. We do not directly administer storage project memberships. Please ensure the relevant Data Owner/Data Manager has added the students through:

  https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/

For the Slurm accounts, we can add the students once we have explicit approval from the account owner (PI). If this has not yet been provided, could the PI please confirm approval?

Once confirmed, we will proceed accordingly.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Storage access must follow Data Owner governance.
- Slurm account additions require PI approval.
- Do not bypass project ownership controls.
- Maintain auditability of account access changes.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / User Management [1][2][3][4][5]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1][2][3][4][5]
Escalation Required: Possibly [1][2][3][4][5]
Urgency: Medium [1][2][3][4][5]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The request involves adding users to both RDS and SL2/SL3 accounts. The RDS addition can be handled through the Self Service Storage Portal, while SL2/SL3 account additions require approval from the Principal Investigator (PI) [1][2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Direct the user to the Self Service Storage Portal for RDS addition: https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/ [1][2][3][4][5].
2. Seek approval from Prof. Cengiz Oztireli for adding the students to the SL2/SL3 accounts [1][2][3][4][5].
3. Once approval is obtained, proceed with adding the students to the specified SL2/SL3 accounts [1][2][3][4][5].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Zhilin,

To add Boqiao Zhang (bz317) and Oszkar Urban (ou222) to the RDS disk `rds-posecraft-5SVx1x5hoTY`, please use the Self Service Storage Portal:
https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/ [1][2][3][4][5]

For adding the students to Prof. Cengiz Oztireli's SL2/SL3 accounts, we need his approval. Please ask Prof. Oztireli to confirm the addition via email.

Once we receive the approval, we will proceed with adding the students to the following accounts:
- OZTIRELI-SL2-CPU
- OZTIRELI-SL2-GPU
- OZTIRELI-SL3-CPU
- OZTIRELI-SL3-GPU

Please let us know if you have any further questions.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not add users to SL2/SL3 accounts without explicit approval from the PI [1][2][3][4][5].
- Use the Self Service Storage Portal for RDS additions to ensure proper management [1][2][3][4][5].

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: AIRR / Authentication / Keycloak (External User)
Technical Action Required: Yes (local Keycloak account creation)
Escalation Required: Yes (AIRR team)
Urgency: Medium (invitation expiry deadline)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User has been invited to join the “UKAEA Early Adopters” project on AIRR and requests creation of a local Keycloak account.

Key points:
- User is external (UKAEA email).
- Invitation link has an expiry date.
- “Local Keycloak entry” indicates use of the documented “without MyAccessID (last resort)” workflow.
- Account must be created in Keycloak before the user can accept the AIRR invitation via “Sign in with Keycloak”.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm invitation context (internal)
   - Verify invitation exists for the user email.
   - Confirm project name: “UKAEA Early Adopters”.
   - Confirm partition/access level requested.

2) Escalate to AIRR team (if not directly handled by HPC Support)
   - Forward ticket to AIRR administrators.
   - Include:
       • Full name
       • Organisation
       • Email
       • Project/partition name
   - Mark urgency if invitation expiry is near.

3) Create local Keycloak account (AIRR/admin action)
   - Create account using provided email.
   - Trigger password setup email.
   - Ensure account is linked to the appropriate realm.

4) Notify user once action is underway
   - Explain that once the local account has been created, they will receive a password setup email.
   - Advise checking spam/junk folders.
   - Instruct them to:
       a) Set password,
       b) Visit invitation link,
       c) Choose “Sign in with Keycloak”,
       d) Use newly created credentials.

5) Provide documentation link
   - Reference official guidance for “Accessing without MyAccessID (last resort)”.

6) Do NOT:
   - Manually approve the project membership unless that is part of the AIRR workflow.
   - Ask for passwords.
   - Override the invitation process.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Has the invitation expired?
- Have you received the password setup email?
- Are you able to access the invitation link successfully?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: UKAEA Early Adopters – AIRR Access

Hi Waqar,

We will arrange creation of the local Keycloak account needed for this AIRR invitation.

Once the account has been created, you should receive an email to set your password. After that, please visit the invitation link, select “Sign in with Keycloak”, and log in using those credentials.

For reference, please see:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html#accessing-without-myaccessid-last-resort

Please let us know if you encounter any issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not transmit passwords via email.
- Do not request sensitive credentials.
- Ensure external accounts are created only through the approved Keycloak workflow.
- Respect invitation expiry deadlines but do not bypass approval controls.
