user_input:
Hello,
I am trying to submit jobs to run my analysis using my GPU allocation for my project.
It seems that there is a 36 hour wall time limit and max number of jobs running at the same time.
Currently I am setting of my analysis with:
#SBATCH --array=0-99
#SBATCH --time=35:30:00
So I don’t think I can make max use of my allocation. Are you able to advice what I can do for this?
Also can you advice what settings to use in the slurm script if I do not need a GPU?
Many thanks
Sam
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / GPU Scheduling / Walltime Limits
Technical Action Required: No (policy explanation + workflow guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (jobs being cancelled due to time limit)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is concerned that GPU jobs cannot exceed the 36-hour walltime limit and asks how to make best use of the allocation.

Key principles:
- GPU partitions enforce a maximum walltime limit (e.g. 36 hours).
- This is a scheduler policy limit, not a configuration error.
- The limit applies regardless of remaining budgeted GPU hours.
- Allocated GPU hours (account balance) are separate from maximum walltime per job.
- Long-running workflows should use checkpointing and job chaining.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm policy limit
   - Explain that the 36-hour walltime is an enforced scheduler limit.
   - It cannot be overridden per job at the user level.

2) Clarify allocation model
   - GPU hour budget defines total consumption allowed.
   - Walltime limit defines maximum duration of a single job.
   - Having remaining hours does not permit exceeding the per-job limit.

3) Recommend checkpointing (best practice)
   - Modify code to periodically write restart/checkpoint files.
   - Submit jobs so that they resume from the last checkpoint.
   - Use job arrays or chained submissions to continue computation.

4) Optional: suggest job chaining
   - Use dependent jobs:
       sbatch --dependency=afterok:<jobid> next_job.sh
   - Or include automatic resubmission logic if checkpoint detected.

6) Do NOT:
   - Promise walltime extensions.
   - Modify partition policy.
   - Suggest splitting allocation across multiple concurrent long jobs to bypass limits.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (IF NEEDED)
- Which partition/account are you using?
- How long does your full workflow need to run?
- Does your code support checkpoint/restart?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Support for job submissions

Hi Sam,

The 36-hour walltime limit is a scheduler policy on the GPU partition and cannot be extended for individual jobs. Even if you have sufficient GPU hours remaining in your allocation, the maximum runtime per job is still capped.

To run longer workflows, we recommend implementing checkpointing so that your job can resume from the last saved state once the walltime is reached. You can then submit follow-up jobs to continue from the checkpoint.

Please let us know if you would like guidance on setting up checkpointing or job dependencies.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Walltime limits are enforced at scheduler level.
- Allocation balance does not override per-job limits.
- Avoid suggesting policy circumvention.
- Promote checkpoint/restart as standard HPC best practice.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Job Submission / GPU Allocation
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is facing constraints with GPU job submissions due to wall time limits and the maximum number of concurrent jobs. The retrieved documents provide guidance on handling these constraints using array jobs and understanding the QoS policies [1][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Confirm the user's service level and specific QoS policies that apply to their account [3][4][5].
2. Advise the user on using array jobs to manage the maximum number of concurrent jobs [1].
3. Provide guidance on job submission without GPU requirements.

ACTION
Direct the user to:
- Use array jobs to manage the maximum number of concurrent jobs. For example, `#SBATCH --array=0-99%32` to limit to 32 concurrent jobs [1].
- For jobs without GPU requirements, remove the GPU-specific directives from the SLURM script.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Sam,

To make the most of your GPU allocation, you can use array jobs to manage the maximum number of concurrent jobs. For example, you can add the following line to your SLURM script:
```
#SBATCH --array=0-99%32
```
This will allow a maximum of 32 jobs to run concurrently, with the next job starting only after one of the previous 32 has finished [1].

For jobs that do not require a GPU, simply remove the GPU-specific directives from your SLURM script.

Please get back in touch if you encounter any issues or need further assistance.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user's job submissions comply with the QoS policies for their service level [3][4][5].
- Do not provide specific wall time limits without confirming the user's service level and QoS policies.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-62385::chunk::0001
[3] : HPCSSUP-69755::chunk::0000
[4] : HPCSSUP-35991::chunk::0003
[5] : HPCSSUP-15950::chunk::0002

[TICKET_SUMMARY] GPU limit

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Namu,
You can submit a large number of jobs each using 1 GPU and you can set up the order in your submission script. Meaning each job will start only once the previous has finished successfully. You will still be limited to 32 GPU used at any moment so in theory you will be able to have a maximum of 32 jobs (assuming each job requests only 1GPU) running at any time. 
Kind regards,
Avraam

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0002 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Dear Avraam,
Thank you very much.
Which commands in the slurm submission script would allow me to do this?
Best regards
Namu

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0003 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Namu,
One way to do this is with array jobs. If you add this line:
```
#SBATCH --array=0-x%32
```
at the end of the SBATCH directives on your submission script then you script will execute x number of tasks but only 32 tasks will run in parallel at any time. This way you can have the maximum of 32 jobs running and the next job will start only after any of the previous 32 has finished. If you have many (thousands) jobs to run (your x) you can split it in multiple jobs. For example you can do #SBATCH --array=0-500%32 if you want to run 500 tasks in total or #SBATCH --array=0-1000%32 if you want to run 1000 tasks.
Let me know if you have any other questions.
Kind regards,
Avraam

</MESSAGE>

user_input:
Hi,
Just a quick Q please - if I running a process on Wilkes using 1 node and 1 GPU and 1 task but I want to increase the number of CPUs used from 32 to 76, how would I do that?
I only need 1 GPU, but I would like to use 76 CPUs, can I increase the task number to achieve this?
Thank you for the information.
Cheers,
Mel
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / GPU-CPU Resource Ratios / Wilkes3 (Ampere)
Technical Action Required: No (scheduler policy clarification)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is running on Wilkes3 (Ampere) requesting:
- 1 node
- 1 GPU
- 1 task
- Wants to increase CPUs from 32 to 76 while still using only 1 GPU.

On Wilkes3 Ampere nodes, Slurm enforces a CPU-per-GPU ratio:
- Maximum 32 CPUs per GPU.
- This is a scheduler-level constraint.
- Users cannot request arbitrary CPU counts independent of GPU count.

Therefore:
- 1 GPU → maximum 32 CPUs.
- To request ~76 CPUs, the user must request 3 GPUs (32 × 3 = 96 CPUs maximum available).

The scheduler will not allow 76 CPUs with only 1 GPU.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Explain enforced policy
   - Ampere nodes allocate CPUs in proportion to GPUs.
   - 32 CPUs per GPU maximum.
   - This prevents GPU starvation and ensures fair resource use.

2) Calculate requirement
   - 76 CPUs ÷ 32 CPUs per GPU ≈ 2.38 GPUs.
   - Since GPUs are indivisible, user must request 3 GPUs.

3) Provide correct submission guidance
   Example directives:
     #SBATCH --gres=gpu:3
     #SBATCH --cpus-per-task=76
   (Ensure CPUs requested do not exceed 32 × GPUs requested.)

4) Clarify limitations
   - Cannot exceed enforced CPU-per-GPU ratio.
   - Cannot request fractional GPUs.
   - Cannot override scheduler policy.

5) Do NOT:
   - Suggest bypassing CPU limits.
   - Recommend modifying system configuration.
   - Escalate unless a policy exception is formally requested.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Which Wilkes partition are you using?
- Are you using --ntasks or --cpus-per-task?
- Do you require 76 CPUs per task or across tasks?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: CPU request on Wilkes

Hello Mel,

On Wilkes3 (Ampere), Slurm enforces a maximum of 32 CPUs per GPU to avoid starving GPUs of CPU resources. This means that if you request 1 GPU, you can receive at most 32 CPUs.

To obtain approximately 76 CPUs, you would need to request 3 GPUs (since 32 × 3 = 96 CPUs available). GPUs must be requested in whole numbers, so fractional allocations are not possible.

For example:

  #SBATCH --gres=gpu:3
  #SBATCH --cpus-per-task=76

Please let us know if you need help adjusting your submission script.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- CPU-per-GPU ratio is scheduler-enforced.
- Do not imply the limit can be overridden.
- Avoid suggesting inefficient resource usage beyond policy.
- Ensure guidance aligns with documented Wilkes3 resource allocation rules.

Here is an example of how to modify your job script:

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RFS / Access Method
Technical Action Required: No (guidance + possible storage conversion request)
Escalation Required: Yes (if user requests NFS-RFS conversion)
Urgency: Low–Medium (workflow clarification)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is attempting to locate an RFS (Research File Share) project under /rfs/project/ on HPC and assumes it should be accessible similarly to RDS. This is incorrect.

Standard RFS storage:
- Is not directly mounted on CSD3/HPC compute or login nodes.
- Is accessed via OS-level mounting from institutional machines (SMB on Windows, SMB/NFS on macOS/Linux).
- Cannot be accessed through the HPC filesystem unless converted to NFS-RFS.

User’s intended workflow:
- Active work on RDS
- Backed-up storage on RFS
- Copy between RDS and RFS

This requires clarification of storage models and possibly a request to convert the RFS project to NFS-RFS.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Clarify storage separation
   Explain that:
   - RDS = high-performance Lustre storage mounted on HPC under /rds
   - RFS = Research File Share (backed-up institutional storage)
   - Standard RFS is not accessible from HPC

2) Answer direct question
   The path does not exist on HPC because RFS is not mounted there.
   It must be accessed via:
   - Windows/macOS mount instructions
   - SMB/NFS mount from a workstation

3) Provide supported HPC-access option
   If user needs RFS accessible from HPC, instruct:
   - Request conversion to NFS-RFS
   - Provide documentation link (RFS on CSD3 – NFS-RFS)
   - Clarify that NFS-RFS allows HPC-side mounting but may not offer RDS-level performance

4) Clarify common workflow
   Typical pattern:
   - Active datasets → RDS
   - Backed-up archival data → RFS or RCS
   - Copy data between systems as required
   - NFS-RFS if active + backed-up + HPC access needed

5) If user confirms NFS-RFS is desired
   - Ask them to email support@hpc.cam.ac.uk with:
       Project name
       Project ID
       Confirmation they want RFS converted to NFS-RFS
   - Escalate to storage team

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Do you require the RFS space to be mounted directly on HPC?
- Is this for active HPC workflows or archival/backup only?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Path to RFS storage

Hello Heather,

RFS storage is not directly mounted on the HPC filesystem, which is why you cannot find it under /rfs/project/.

Standard RFS is accessed by mounting it from your local machine (e.g. via SMB). It is not available on CSD3 login or compute nodes by default.

If you require your RFS project to be accessible from HPC, you can request that it be converted to NFS-RFS. This allows it to be mounted on CSD3, although performance characteristics differ from RDS.

Please see:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rfs/accessing-rfs/rfs-nfs.html

If you would like to proceed with conversion to NFS-RFS, please email support@hpc.cam.ac.uk with the project name and ID and we can arrange this for you.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply RFS is mounted on HPC by default.
- Do not suggest manual mounting inside compute jobs.
- Do not guarantee performance equivalence between RDS and NFS-RFS.
- Escalate only if conversion request is explicitly confirmed.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Path Validation
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is unable to locate their RFS folder on HPC. The retrieved documents suggest that RFS folders are typically located under `/rfs/project/` but may also have local copies in the user's home directory [2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the existence of the RFS folder at `/rfs/project/rfs-hem48-storageactive`.
2. Check for any local copies of the RFS folder in the user's home directory.
3. If the folder is not found, escalate to the RCS team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm if you have checked the path `/rfs/project/rfs-hem48-storageactive`?
- Have you looked for any local copies of the RFS folder in your home directory?
- Please provide the output of `ls -l ~/rfs` and `readlink -f /rfs/project/rfs-hem48-storageactive`.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Heather,

Thank you for your message. The RFS folders are typically located under `/rfs/project/`. However, there might be local copies in your home directory.

Please check the following path:
`/rfs/project/rfs-hem48-storageactive`

If you cannot find the folder, please provide the output of the following commands:
`ls -l ~/rfs`
`readlink -f /rfs/project/rfs-hem48-storageactive`

We will investigate further if the folder is not found.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not guess a target path without verified evidence [2][3].
- Escalate to the RCS team if the folder is not found in the expected locations.

I am encountering an MPI-related runtime error when attempting to use the gmx_mpi executable from the gromacs/2024.5 module on the pvc9 partition. Here is a job submission script as a minimal example to reproduce the issue:
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=LSN_prep
#SBATCH --partition=pvc9

. /etc/profile.d/modules.sh

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: MPI / Slurm Environment / Dawn (PVC9)
Technical Action Required: No (user-side environment correction)
Escalation Required: No (unless issue persists after environment fix)
Urgency: Medium (job fails in batch mode)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user reports an MPI bootstrap failure when running:

    mpirun -np 1 gmx_mpi -h

within a batch job on the pvc9 partition.

Error excerpt:
    HYD_spawn ... execvp error on file /usr/bin/srun (No such file or directory)

Key observations:
- The job works correctly in an interactive session launched via `srun`.
- The batch script manually purges modules and loads a custom module path.
- The error indicates Intel MPI’s Hydra bootstrap cannot locate `srun`.

Most likely cause:
The batch script is not loading the correct Dawn node environment (RHEL9 default stack), so Slurm integration and related paths (including srun) are not correctly initialised inside the job environment.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Ensure correct base environment is loaded
   Instruct user to add:

       module load rhel9/default-dawn

   immediately after `module purge` in their submission script.

   This ensures:
   - Correct RHEL9 runtime environment
   - Proper Slurm integration
   - Required system paths (including srun)
   - Compatible MPI environment

2) Recommend simplified module order
   Example structure:

       module purge
       module load rhel9/default-dawn
       module use /usr/local/dawn/software/spack-rocky9/spack-modules/dawn-2025-03-23/linux-rocky9-sapphirerapids
       module load gromacs/2024.5

   Avoid mixing incompatible stacks unless required.

3) Retest with minimal command
   After modification, test again with:

       mpirun -np 1 gmx_mpi -h

4) If issue persists, request:
   - Full submission script
   - Complete stdout and stderr files
   - Output of:
         which mpirun
         which srun
         module list

EXPECTED SIGNAL AFTER FIX
- No Hydra bootstrap error.
- mpirun executes successfully.
- gmx_mpi help output prints normally.

BRANCH: If still failing
- Confirm Intel MPI is correctly integrated with Slurm.
- Verify that `srun` exists in PATH within job:
      echo $PATH
      ls -l /usr/bin/srun
- Escalate to platform admins only if Slurm binary is genuinely unavailable on compute node (unlikely).

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: MPI-related runtime error on pvc9

Hello Wei-Tse,

It looks like the Dawn node environment is not being fully initialised in your batch job.

Please add the following line after `module purge` in your submission script:

  module load rhel9/default-dawn

This loads the correct RHEL9 runtime and Slurm integration required for MPI jobs on pvc9.

After adding this, please resubmit the job and let us know if the issue persists. If so, please send the full submission script and output files.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not suggest installing MPI manually.
- Do not recommend modifying system Slurm paths.
- Avoid blaming firewall or host availability unless independently verified.
- Keep troubleshooting limited to supported module stacks.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / MPI Error
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is encountering an MPI-related runtime error when attempting to use the `gmx_mpi` executable from the `gromacs/2024.5` module on the `pvc9` partition. The error message indicates issues with the `hydra_bstrap_proxy` and `srun` executable. Similar issues have been reported in previous tickets [1][2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the availability and permissions of the `hydra_bstrap_proxy` on all nodes.
2. Check the firewall settings to ensure enough ports are allowed and specify them with the `I_MPI_PORT_RANGE` variable if necessary.
3. Test the job submission script with alternative launchers using the `-bootstrap` option.
4. If the issue persists, escalate to the system administrators for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm if the `hydra_bstrap_proxy` is available on all nodes and has the correct permissions?
- Have you checked the firewall settings to ensure enough ports are allowed?
- Have you tried using the `-bootstrap` option to select an alternative launcher?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Wei-Tse,

Thank you for your detailed report. The error you are encountering is related to the MPI setup and has been observed in similar cases [1][2]. Here are some steps to troubleshoot the issue:

1. Verify the availability and permissions of the `hydra_bstrap_proxy` on all nodes.
2. Check the firewall settings to ensure enough ports are allowed and specify them with the `I_MPI_PORT_RANGE` variable if necessary.
3. Test the job submission script with alternative launchers using the `-bootstrap` option.

If the issue persists, we may need to escalate this to our system administrators for further investigation.

Please let us know if you have any questions or need further assistance.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not perform manual changes to system configurations without proper authorization.
- Ensure all troubleshooting steps are documented for future reference.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-41381::chunk::0002
[2] : HPCSSUP-60143::chunk::0002

CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS / Quota Diagnosis
Technical Action Required: No (user action required to reduce file count)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (job failing due to quota)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports `Disk quota exceeded` errors while writing files to an RDS project, despite not obviously expecting the project to be full. On RDS, this can happen either because the space quota is exhausted or because the inode/file-count quota has been reached. From the initial report alone, inode exhaustion is a strong possibility, especially given the shell errors creating temporary files, but `quota` output is needed to confirm whether the problem is space usage or file count.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Confirm which quota has been exhausted
   Ask the user to run:
     quota
   and check the relevant RDS project entry.
   - If the GB column is at or above the limit, this is a space-quota problem.
   - If the files column is at the limit or marked with an asterisk (*), this is an inode/file-count problem.

2) Explain the distinction between storage quota and inode quota
   - Storage quota = total disk space (GB/TB)
   - Inode quota = total number of files
   - Hitting either limit blocks new file creation

3) Provide remediation options

   Option A — Reduce file count (recommended if inode limit reached)
   - Delete unnecessary small files
   - Consolidate many small files into archives (e.g. tar)
   - Combine image files into fewer container formats if possible

   Example:
     tar -czf archive_episodes_12000_12100.tar.gz episode_0120*

   Option B — Increase storage allocation
   - Inode quota increases only when total RDS storage allocation increases.
   - This requires purchasing additional storage capacity.
   - Refer to RDS quota documentation.

4) Clarify policy
   - Inode quotas are fixed per TiB.
   - They cannot be increased independently of storage allocation.

5) Close once the user understands which limit was hit and how to proceed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Subject: Re: Quota exceeded on RDS project

Hello Liyou,

The `Disk quota exceeded` message can be caused either by running out of space or by reaching the inode/file-count limit on the RDS project.

Please run:

  quota

and send us the output for the affected RDS project. If the limit has been hit in the `files` column rather than the GB column, that would indicate an inode quota issue rather than lack of storage space.

If it is an inode limit, the usual remedies are to remove or consolidate many small files or to increase the total storage allocation.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply a filesystem fault without first confirming whether space or inode quota has been reached.
- Do not suggest modifying system quota settings directly.
- Inode quotas are proportional to purchased storage and are not adjustable independently.
- Avoid recommending unsafe bulk deletion commands.

Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is encountering disk quota exceeded errors despite having a 2TB allocation on RDS. The issue is not isolated and has been reported by other users in similar contexts [1][2][3][4][5]. The error messages indicate that the quota is being exceeded, which is unusual given the user's reported allocation.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the user's current disk usage and quota on the RDS.
2. Check for any hidden or temporary files that might be consuming space.
3. Review the user's job submission script for any potential issues related to file handling or temporary storage.
4. If necessary, escalate to the storage team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the output of the `quota` command?
- Are there any hidden or temporary files in your working directory?
- Have you recently made any changes to your job submission script or workflow?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Liyou,

Thank you for providing the details. It appears that you are encountering disk quota exceeded errors despite having a 2TB allocation on RDS. This issue has been reported by other users in similar contexts [1][2][3][4][5].

To help us diagnose the problem, could you please provide the output of the `quota` command? Additionally, please check for any hidden or temporary files in your working directory that might be consuming space.

If you have recently made any changes to your job submission script or workflow, please let us know.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Always verify the user's current disk usage and quota before making any changes.
- Ensure that any escalation to the storage team is done with all relevant information provided.

Dear Zakaria,
Your /home/zm297 and /home/zm297/rds/hpc-work are separate file systems. First one has only 40GB of space and rds has 1TB.  You can type 'quota' to see your usage. You can move your data to hpc-work and run it from there to fix this.
 
Kind regards,
Greg
RCS Team

[TICKET_SUMMARY] repeated job failures, disk quota exceeded?

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear support,  
I've been having trouble with getting jobs to complete. I've returned from a break, but all simulations I set up seem to have failed with a few different exit codes; 1, 255, 38 mostly.  
I'm using Castep and its magres module to calculate NMR parameters on very similar systems. As a results, most input files are identical to previously successful jobs.   
Exit codes of 1 and 255 have a line similar to this in the log. 
> slurmstepd: error: task_p_post_term: rmdir(/sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/slurm46024470/slurm46024470.4294967294_0) failed Device or resource busy
>  
 
Exit codes of 38 have this in the log. 
 
>  forrtl: Disk quota exceeded 
> forrtl: severe (38): error during write, unit 10, file /rds/user/djm254/hpc-work/KCl/3xKCl/magres/800K/1000/KCl.$ 
> Image              PC                Routine            Line        Source 
> castep.mpi         0000000007FDD363  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown 
> castep.mpi         000000000804076F  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown 
> castep.mpi         000000000803C01B  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown 
> castep.mpi         0000000003CFC9E7  license_mp_licens         314  license.F90 
> castep.mpi         000000000040A525  MAIN__.a                  161  castep.f90 
> castep.mpi         000000000040A21E  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown 
> libc-2.17.so       00002B56602FD555  __libc_start_main     Unknown  Unknown 
> castep.mpi         000000000040A129  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown 
 
Is this indicative some read/write quota has been reached? Do you have a suggested fix?  
As there were about 100 jobs, I've generalised rather than provide locations of each job.  
User djm254 
acc FARNAN-SL3-CPU  
submission script (e.g.; differs in nodes, tasks and time for each job): 
 
>  #!/bin/bash 
> #SBATCH --mail-user=<djm254@cam.ac.uk> 
> #SBATCH --job-name=800K1000psKClheat 
> #SBATCH --account FARNAN-SL3-CPU 
> #SBATCH --partition skylake 
> #SBATCH --nodes 12 
> #SBATCH --ntasks 384 
> #SBATCH --time 3:00:00 
> #SBATCH --mail-type=START,FAIL,END 
> #SBATCH --output=%u-%j.log 
> export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/local/software/rdma-core/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH 
> module purge 
> module load rhel7/default-peta4 castep 
> mpirun castep KCl
   
 
Daniel 
> Daniel-James Maguire MChem (Hons) MRes DICPhD CandidateDepartment of Earth SciencesUniversity of CambridgeDowning Street, Cambridge, CB2 3EQdjm254@cam.ac.uk

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Procurement / Pricing Information
Technical HPC Action Required: No
Finance Action Required: No (information only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting a formal quote for:
- Cumulus CPU 200,000 hours at £0.01/CPU hour
- Research Data Store (RDS) 10 TB for 3 years at £162/TB

HPC Support does not issue formal quotes. Pricing information is published and departments must raise purchase orders via their own finance processes.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Clarify that formal quotes are not issued.
2) Confirm the published pricing model (if appropriate).
3) Advise that departmental finance teams should proceed using the published rates.
4) Do not generate bespoke quotation documents.
5) Do not raise purchase orders on behalf of departments.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Quote request for Cumulus CPU and RDS

Dear Dave,

Thank you for your message.

We do not issue formal quotation documents for Cumulus CPU time or RDS storage. The services are charged according to the published pricing, and your departmental finance team can proceed based on those rates.

For reference:
- Cumulus CPU usage is charged per CPU hour at the published rate.
- RDS storage is charged per TB per year according to the current pricing schedule.

If your department requires confirmation of the published pricing, this is available on the HPC documentation pages.

Please let us know if you have any further questions.

Best regards,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not generate unofficial quote documents.
- Do not commit to pricing beyond the published rates.
- Do not raise purchase orders on behalf of users.
- Ensure any pricing referenced aligns with the current published documentation.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Purchasing / Quote Request
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user requires quotes for purchasing Cumulus CPU hours and Research Data Store (RDS) storage. The retrieved documents indicate that quotes for RDS storage can be generated via the self-service portal, while CPU hours can be quoted via email.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Direct the user to the self-service portal for generating a quote for RDS storage.
2. Provide an email quote for the Cumulus CPU hours.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Dave,

For the Research Data Store (RDS) storage, you can generate a quote using the self-service portal:
https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/RDS/ [3][4]

For the Cumulus CPU hours, please accept this as an email quote for the PO that you are raising:
200,000 Service Level 2 CPU hours at a unit rate of £0.01, total cost of £2000. Owning PI is Prof. David Klenerman (dk10012).

Let me know if you require anything else.

Kind regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that quotes are provided accurately and in line with the documented prices [1][2][3][4][5].
- Direct users to the self-service portal for RDS storage quotes to maintain consistency and accuracy [3][4].

user_input:
Dear HPC Team,
Could we add a few more recent CUDA modules to the cluster? At the moment I can load cuda/12.1, but many current DL packages (e.g., recent PyTorch/TensorFlow wheels) target CUDA 12.4 or 12.6. Having cuda/12.4 and cuda/12.6available would greatly improve compatibility and avoid building from source.
If feasible, installing these alongside the existing cuda/12.1 (plus matching cuDNN where applicable) would be ideal. I’m happy to test the new modules once they’re deployed.
Many thanks!
Best regards,
Yijiang Dong
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software Stack / CUDA Module Availability
Technical Action Required: No (guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requests CUDA 12.4/12.6 modules for package compatibility. CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 are not officially supported, and CUDA 12.8.1 is available in the Ampere test stack.

ACTION
1) Clarify that CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 are not currently supported.
2) Provide test stack commands for CUDA 12.8.1.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Yijiang,

We do not currently provide official CUDA 12.4 or 12.6 modules. We do, however, have a newer test software stack that includes CUDA 12.8.1.

Please run:

module purge
module load rhel8/slurm
module load rhel8/global
module use /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/spack-modules/test/ampere-2025-06-01/linux-rocky8-zen3
module load cuda/12.8.1/gcc/kdeps6ab

Please let us know if you run into any issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not promise unsupported module versions.
- Distinguish test stack from officially supported stack.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / Module Installation
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Yes (RSE Team)
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user has requested the installation of CUDA versions 12.4 and 12.6, along with matching cuDNN versions where applicable. Similar requests for newer CUDA versions have been made in the past and were escalated to the RSE team [1][2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Escalate the request to the RSE team for the installation of CUDA 12.4 and CUDA 12.6, along with matching cuDNN versions.
2. Inform the user that their request has been forwarded to the RSE team for further action.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Yijiang,

Thank you for your request. We have forwarded your request for the installation of CUDA 12.4 and CUDA 12.6, along with matching cuDNN versions, to our RSE team. We will keep you updated on the progress.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that all software installation requests are properly escalated to the RSE team for review and implementation [1][2][3].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-99885::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-98682::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-76188::chunk::0001
retrieved_contexts:
[TICKET_SUMMARY] Request for CUDA versions

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear CSD3 Support Team,
I am writing to enquire whether it would be possible to install more recent versions of CUDA on the cluster. At present, the latest version available via module avail is 12.1; however, my research depends on features introduced in later releases (12.4, 12.6, 12.8).
Thank you very much for your support.
Kind regards,
Yuval

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Cuda12.2

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hello,
I hope you are well.
I am trying to install and compile a package on csd3 which requires cuda12.2 and I am aware that the cluster only has up to cuda12.1. I am therefore wondering if it is by any chance possible to have newer versions of cuda as a module on the cluster?
Thank you!
Best,
Janice

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Need CUDA 12.2

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Xiaochen,

CUDA/12.1 is available on CSD3, please use the following command to load cuda/12.1.

```
module load cuda/12.1
```
Kind regards,
John Metson

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0002 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Dear John,
Thanks for replying. Yes, I understand that we have cuda 12.1. However, we are trying to replicate the results and develop on a third-party system for distributed training that only supports cuda 12.2. Could you please add 12.2 to the modules available?
Thank you very much.
Best Wishes,
Xiaochen

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0003 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Xiaochen,

I will pass this request onto our RSE team.

Kind regards,
John Metson

</MESSAGE>

[TICKET_SUMMARY] cuDNN Module

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hi HPC team,
Would it be possible to add support for the latest version of cuDNN (8.3.1) via the modules available on [login-gpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk](http://login-gpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk)? It would be fantastic if support for the latest version of CUDA (11.5) could be added as well, but this would be secondary to cuDNN.
Best,
Nicholas Boucher

Best,
Nicholas Boucher

CLASSIFICATION
Category: Remote Access / TurboVNC
Technical Action Required: No (usage correction)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User launched VNC on a login node and attempted local port forward, causing binding/forwarding issues. Correct method is to run VNC on an interactive GPU node (e.g. `*-r-gpu`) following documented workflow.

ACTION
1) Instruct user to create VNC session on an interactive node, not login node.
2) Share the VNC guide and expected command sequence.
3) Confirm resolution after user retest.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Iryna,

Please create your TurboVNC session on an interactive GPU node (`*-r-gpu`) rather than a login node. That should resolve the forwarding issue you’re seeing.

Please follow the documented TurboVNC workflow for interactive GPU nodes, and let us know if you run into further issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not encourage running long-lived GUI sessions on login nodes.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / TurboVNC
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is encountering an error when attempting to establish an SSH tunnel for TurboVNC. The error message indicates a failure to bind to the specified port. This issue is commonly related to port conflicts or incorrect port forwarding commands [1][2][3][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Confirm the user's steps and commands used for starting the VNC server and establishing the SSH tunnel.
2. Verify if the port specified in the SSH command is free and not in use.
3. Provide guidance on checking for free ports and correcting the SSH command if necessary.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- What is the exact command used to start the VNC server?
- What is the exact SSH command used to establish the tunnel?
- Are there any other applications or services running on the specified port?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Iryna,

Thank you for reaching out. The error you're encountering suggests a port conflict or incorrect port forwarding command. Please follow these steps to resolve the issue:

1. Ensure the VNC server is started correctly. The command should look something like this:
   ```
   /opt/TurboVNC/bin/vncserver :9 -depth 24 -geometry 1910x1000
   ```

2. Verify that the port specified in the SSH command is free and not in use. You can check for free ports using the `pgrep -a Xvnc` command.

3. Use the following SSH command to establish the tunnel:
   ```
   ssh -L 5909:localhost:5909 iv285@login-q-3.hpc.cam.ac.uk
   ```

If the issue persists, please provide the exact commands used and any additional error messages.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user follows the correct steps for starting the VNC server and establishing the SSH tunnel as per the documentation [1][2][3][4].
- Do not guess or provide commands without verifying the user's steps and context.

user_input:
Dear support team,
I need to rsync a large dataset from our rcs project (cc234). Doing it from a login node is probably not the way, as I'm being kicked out, I guess because the rsync is too heavy. Is there a dedicated data transfer node I can ssh to, or should I do it via an interactive job?
Thanks,
Yaniv
Sent from Outlook for Android<[https://aka.ms/AAb9ysg](https://aka.ms/AAb9ysg)>
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RCS Data Transfer
Technical Action Required: No (best-practice guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is running heavy `rsync` from login node and getting disconnected. Recommended approach is session persistence (screen/tmux) or GUI transfer workflow; follow RCS transfer best practices.

ACTION
1) Recommend using `screen`/`tmux` for long transfer sessions.
2) Optionally direct to supported GUI transfer methods for RCS.
3) Share RCS best-practice guidance.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Yaniv,

For long RCS transfers, please use a persistent terminal session (for example `screen`/`tmux`) so the transfer can continue if your SSH session drops.

You can also use a supported GUI transfer method:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rcs/gui.html

Best-practice guidance:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rcs/best-practice.html

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Avoid advising large unsupervised transfers directly from transient login sessions.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Data Transfer
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
RCS is not mounted on the CSD3 compute nodes, only on the login nodes. Therefore, rsync should be run from a login node. Using a screen session can help prevent disconnections during long transfers [2][3].

ACTION
Direct the user to run rsync from a login node and use a screen session to prevent disconnections.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Yaniv,

RCS is not mounted on the CSD3 compute nodes, only on the login nodes. Therefore, you should run rsync from a login node. To prevent disconnections during long transfers, you can use a screen session.

Here is an example command for rsync:
```
rsync -avz /path/to/source /path/to/destination
```

For more information on using rsync, please refer to our documentation:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rds/cli.html

Please get back in touch if you encounter any issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not attempt to run rsync from compute nodes as RCS is not mounted there [2][3].
- Use screen sessions to prevent disconnections during long transfers [2][3].

REFERENCE KEY
[2] : HPCSSUP-54593::chunk::0002
[3] : HPCSSUP-51249::chunk::0004

e,
RCS is not mounted on the CSD3 compute nodes only the login nodes. This means that you can only access it from the login nodes and not from any job that runs in a compute node. Did you have any problems transferring data from RFS to RCS from a login node and that's why you tried to submit a job?
Kind regards,
Avraam

[TICKET_SUMMARY] accessing RCS storage

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hi,
I have a large amount of data stored on RCS which I would like to access 
- I want to transfer or copy a series of directories one at a time to my 
RDS storage, run some software and then move back to RCS. Please could 
you let me know the best way to do this? Is just using rsync and running 
in the background on a login node OK? I tend to lose my connection quite 
often so I'd prefer not to need to be actively connected.
Thanks very much,
Katy

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Rsync errors - RFS to RCS

[CONTEXT]
e,
RCS is not mounted on the CSD3 compute nodes only the login nodes. This means that you can only access it from the login nodes and not from any job that runs in a compute node. Did you have any problems transferring data from RFS to RCS from a login node and that's why you tried to submit a job?
Kind regards,
Avraam

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0003 role=TICKET_CREATOR>

Thanks for your quick response. 
I tried to submit using a slurm job initially as I thought this was best practice and I’m pretty sure I did this previously and it worked? 
However it isn’t working at the moment. 
  
On the other hand, when I do it directly on command line (I assume this is login node) it works perfectly. However, this takes a while and I am moving a lot of data – another reason why I wanted to submit a slurm job.  
  
Should I just continue to do it manually for every file on command line?
Many thankss 
Natalie

user_input:
Hi,
We are having some issues with various ceph shares on the Arcus cloud 
today and yesterday. Seeing metadata servers switch to reconnecting e.g.:
> a900cf30-f8a3-42bf-98d6-af7ce92f1a1a.client1188705722/mds_sessions
> ::::::::::::::
> global_id 1188705722
> name "iris-gaia-code-spec-ro"
> mds.1 reconnecting
> a900cf30-f8a3-42bf-98d6-af7ce92f1a1a.client1188705717/mds_sessions
> ::::::::::::::
> global_id 1188705717
> name "process-space-1"
> mds.1 reconnecting
This is causing filesystem access to hang.
Rebooting the instances seems to be clearing this in some cases but it 
is happening on multiple instances and also recurring. Is there any 
indication of an underlying issue?
Cheers,
Patrick.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / CephFS / MDS connectivity (Arcus cloud)
Technical Action Required: Yes (service-side investigation/mitigation)
Escalation Required: Yes (Ceph/storage administrators + network)
Urgency: High (filesystem hangs across multiple instances)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports Ceph shares on Arcus cloud hanging across multiple instances, with client session state showing repeated MDS reconnecting behaviour and filesystem access hanging. Because the issue affects multiple shares and instances, this is more consistent with a service-side CephFS/MDS or network-path problem than a single-instance misconfiguration. Rebooting instances may temporarily clear symptoms but should not be treated as the primary fix.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Acknowledge and treat as a service incident
   - Confirm the issue affects multiple instances/shares.
   - Prioritise service-side checks over detailed user-side debugging.

2) Collect minimal high-signal diagnostics from the user
   Ask for:
   - example affected instance name(s)
   - approximate timestamps of failures
   - exact mount command(s) or error output if readily available

3) Service-side investigation (storage/admin)
   Check:
   - Ceph cluster health summary and recent warnings
   - CephFS filesystem status and active/standby MDS state
   - MDS daemon logs around the failure window
   - client session reconnect/eviction behaviour
   - any ongoing rebalancing or recovery that could affect metadata availability

4) Network path verification (admin)
   Verify routing/interface configuration and any recent changes affecting CephFS traffic.

5) Ask users to retry only after backend checks or remediation have been completed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Patrick,

Thanks for the detailed report. Because the hangs are affecting multiple instances and shares, this looks more like a service-side CephFS issue than a single-instance configuration problem.

We’re investigating the backend now. In the meantime, could you please share:
- the names of one or two affected instances,
- the approximate timestamps of the most recent failures,
- and one example of the mount command or error output you are seeing?

Once we have checked the backend state, we can advise whether any instance-side action is still needed.

Cheers,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Treat multi-instance CephFS hangs as a service incident; avoid shifting burden to users.
- Avoid making claims about root cause unless supported by service-side evidence.
- Suggest disruptive workarounds (instance reboot) only with explicit user confirmation and after backend stability is restored.
- Do not request or expose sensitive infrastructure details in user-facing messages.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Ceph Cluster Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Yes
Urgency: High

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The issue described is similar to previous incidents where Ceph metadata servers (MDS) were experiencing connectivity problems, causing filesystem access to hang [1][2]. This is a recurring issue that has affected multiple instances and may indicate an underlying problem with the Arcus Ceph cluster.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Escalate the issue to the Arcus cloud support team for investigation.
2. Inform the user that the issue is being escalated and provide an estimated time for resolution if possible.
3. Monitor the status of the Ceph cluster and update the user as necessary.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Patrick,

Thank you for reporting this issue. We have identified that the problem with the Ceph shares is likely related to the metadata servers experiencing connectivity issues. This is a recurring problem that has been reported in the past [1][2].

We are escalating this issue to the Arcus cloud support team for further investigation. We will keep you updated on the progress and provide an estimated time for resolution as soon as possible.

Please let us know if you have any further questions or concerns.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the issue is properly escalated to the appropriate support team.
- Keep the user informed about the status of the investigation and resolution.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-86663::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-87130::chunk::0000

Dear support team,
Our team wanted to top up some more GPU core times and extra rds disk spaces for project 'QAKG-SL2-GPU'? Could you please give us a quote for the price of 10000 extra GPU hours and 20 GB extra rds space?
Best wishes,
Wenyu
The University of Edinburgh is a charitable body, registered in Scotland, with registration number SC005336. Is e buidheann carthannais a th' ann an Oilthigh Dh?n ?ideann, cl?raichte an Alba, ?ireamh cl?raidh SC005336.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: CORE Procurement / Quote Clarification
Technical Action Required: No (quote preparation and clarification)
Finance Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is requesting pricing for 10,000 additional GPU hours and additional RDS storage on `QAKG-SL2-GPU`. Because the message says `20 GB`, which is unusually small in this procurement context, the safest response is to confirm the intended storage amount and whether the quote should cover only new storage or also any existing storage renewal/extension before issuing a final figure.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Clarify the storage request before quoting
   - Confirm whether `20 GB` is correct or a typo.
   - Confirm whether the user means additional capacity on top of the current licensed storage.
   - Confirm whether the quote should cover only extra storage or also renewal/extension of existing storage.

2) Quote the GPU hours separately using the current applicable rate.

3) Once the storage details are confirmed, issue a written email quote covering both the GPU hours and the corrected storage amount.

4) Ask the user to send the PO to the ticket once ready.

5) Keep any later account-creation request separate from the pricing calculation.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Dear Wenyu,

Before I finalise the storage part of the quote, could you please confirm whether `20 GB` is correct? That is unusually small for this service, so I want to make sure we quote the right storage amount and whether this is additional capacity or an extension of your current storage.

Once you confirm the intended storage quantity and scope, I can provide the full written quote for both storage and GPU hours.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not silently convert user-supplied units.
- If the storage quantity looks implausible, ask before quoting.
- Keep quote assumptions explicit, especially if renewal or extension of existing storage affects cost.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Resource Allocation / Top-Up Request
Technical Action Required: No
Portal Action Required: Yes
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting a quote for additional GPU core hours and RDS disk space for their project. Previous tickets show that top-up requests are processed with purchase orders [2][3][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Inform the user that top-up requests require a purchase order.
2. Direct the user to provide a purchase order for the requested resources.
3. Do not process the request without a valid purchase order.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Wenyu,

To process your request for additional GPU core hours and RDS disk space, we will need a purchase order. Please provide the purchase order for the requested resources.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not process top-up requests without a valid purchase order [2][3][4].

REFERENCE KEY
[2] : HPCSSUP-43312::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-57765::chunk::0000
[4] : HPCSSUP-44422::chunk::0000

Hello,
I am having problems logging in to hpc via ssh irs38@login-gpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk as usual, even though an email has been sent out confirming the end of the maintenance work. I just wanted to check if there isn't something I am missing, or if it is just taking time until hpc will be fully available.
Kind regards,
Ieva
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Service Availability / Post-Maintenance
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports inability to SSH shortly after maintenance completion notice. Correct response is that services may still be coming online; direct users to service status and communications.

ACTION
1) Inform user service restoration is still in progress.
2) Provide status page and advise monitoring updates.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Ieva,

We are still bringing services online following maintenance. Please keep an eye on service communications and the status page for updates:
https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/service-status

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not declare full service restoration until confirmed.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: HPC / Login Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues logging into the HPC via SSH after maintenance. Similar issues have been reported in the past, often due to ongoing maintenance or post-maintenance delays [1][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the current status of the HPC nodes to ensure maintenance is complete.
2. Check for any ongoing issues that might be affecting login-gpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk.
3. If the issue persists, escalate to the technical team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm if you are using the correct UIS password and TOTP code labeled as “CSD3:SSH Login” in your authenticator app? [1]
- Have you tried logging into an alternative node, such as login-icelake.hpc.cam.ac.uk? [5]

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Ieva,

Thank you for your message. We are aware that there was recent maintenance on the HPC nodes. Please ensure you are using the correct UIS password and TOTP code labeled as “CSD3:SSH Login” in your authenticator app.

If the issue persists, please try logging into an alternative node, such as login-icelake.hpc.cam.ac.uk.

We will verify the current status of the HPC nodes and get back to you if there are any ongoing issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user is informed about any ongoing maintenance or delays.
- Do not provide login credentials or sensitive information via email.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-90471::chunk::0002
[3] : HPCSSUP-66727::chunk::0001
[4] : HPCSSUP-92106::chunk::0000
[5] : HPCSSUP-71354::chunk::0001

Connection closed by 128.232.224.41 port 22" after password and TOTP code input

user_input:
Hi HPC Support Team,
I noticed that my home directory does not have a symlink ~/rfs pointing to the RFS project folder. Currently, I can access the project data using the absolute path /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw, but having the symlink would make it much more convenient.
Could you please create the ~/rfs symlink in my home directory?
Thank you so much for your help!
Best regards,
Hansheng
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Filesystem / Symlink / Home Quota
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User wants a convenience symlink from `~/rfs` to `/rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw`. The correct command is straightforward, but the ticket shows the attempt failed because the user's `/home` directory was already over quota. In that case the symlink itself cannot be created until home usage is reduced.

ACTION
1) Ask the user to create the link with the concrete command:
   - `cd $HOME`
   - `ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw $HOME/rfs`
2) If they see `No space left on device`, ask for `quota` output.
3) Explain that the immediate problem is `/home` usage above the 50 GB quota, not an RFS permission problem.
4) Advise them to remove unneeded files from `/home` or move suitable data to `hpc-work`.
5) Mention that accidentally deleted files from `/home` may be recoverable from `~/.snapshot`.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Hansheng,

Please try:

  cd $HOME
  ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw $HOME/rfs

If you receive `No space left on device`, please run `quota` and send us the output. That would usually indicate your `/home` directory is full, which would prevent creation of even a small symlink entry.

If your home usage is over quota, you will need to move some data into `hpc-work` or delete unwanted files from `/home` before retrying. If you remove anything from `/home` by mistake, you can usually restore it from `~/.snapshot`.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Use the concrete path from the user request when it is already known.
- Distinguish `/home` quota exhaustion from RFS access problems.
- Do not advise deleting RFS data to fix a `/home` quota issue.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Symlink Creation
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user needs a symlink to their RFS project folder in their home directory. This can be done by the user following the instructions provided in previous tickets [1][2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Provide the user with the commands to create the symlink [1][2][3].
2. Ask the user to confirm if the symlink creation was successful [1][2].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Hansheng,

You can create the symlink to your RFS project folder by running the following commands in your home directory:

```
cd
mkdir -p ~/rfs
cd rfs
ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw teichlab-nfs
```

Please try these commands and let us know if you encounter any issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure the user has the correct permissions to create symlinks in their home directory [1][2][3].
- Verify the UUID and project name before providing the commands to the user [1][2][3].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-89742::chunk::0002
[2] : HPCSSUP-89207::chunk::0002
[3] : HPCSSUP-89614::chunk::0001

<MESSAGE id=0004 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Nadav,
If you cd to your home directory, you can create a symlink to this project with 
```
ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-<UUID> <human name>-nfs
```
Where the human name is `teichlab-nfs` and UUID is `iCNyzSAaucw`
Best wishes, 
Henry
HPC Support

Best wishes, 
Henry

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Support for no RFS found for my user

[CONTEXT]
Dear Muhammad,
Any updates on this?
Kind regards, Davod

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0004 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Davod,
It is good news that you were able to access the storage through its absolute path. Now we can create a symbolic link using the following set of commands from your end after entering a login node:
```
cd
mkdir rfs
cd rfs
ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw rfs-iCNyzSAaucw
cd rfs-iCNyzSAaucw
```
Please give it a try. You should be able to access the link to your project folder.
Regards,
Muhammad Ahmed
HPC Services

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0005 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
﻿Dear Muhammad,
Thank you, all working now.
Kind regards, Davod

user_input:
hello,
after upgrading from gfortran-5.4 to gfortran-13.3 my fortran based mpi 
code produced interface related warnings during compile and crashed 
during execution. Replacing 'include mpif.h' statements with 'use mpi' 
statements (plus a few other changes) fixed the problems on my local 
linux box. However, when compiling on CSD3 I get the following mpi.mod 
error:
f951: Fatal Error: Reading module 
'/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/mpi.mod' 
at line 1 column 2: Unexpected EOF
The code is in /home/mh526/Atham/svn/branches/FlAshPlume. The compiler 
settings are in 
/home/mh526/Atham/svn/branches/FlAshPlume/source/Makefile.COMPILE
For the compile I load the following modules:
module load rhel8/cclake/base
module load gcc/13.3.0/7ukda7ns
module load netcdf-fortran-pnetcdf/4.6.1/gcc/intel-oneapi-mpi/ptuvn62k
The compile command  that produces the error is
/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/bin/mpif90 
-c -DMPI -g -O3 -ffree-line-length-none -mcmodel=medium 
-I/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/netcdf-fortran-4.6.1-ptuvn62k6et3sihhin2vrwbtyjuycp4o/include 
-I/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/parallel-netcdf-1.12.3-sn2y3yw32wdavsrvyy6g2dgru3x363d3/include 
/home/mh526/Atham/svn/branches/FlAshPlume/source/Basic/precision.F90
Are you able to tell me what the problem is? Am I using the wrong 
mpi.mod file?
Many thanks,
Michael
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Build/Compiler Toolchain / MPI Fortran Modules
Technical Action Required: Yes (workaround instruction)
Escalation Required: Optional (packaging improvement)
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
`gfortran 13.3` with Intel MPI hit `mpi.mod` read error (`Unexpected EOF`) because Intel MPI Fortran module files in that build were available only for older gfortran ABI versions. Workaround: explicitly include compatible `gfortran/11.1.0` (or `10.2.0`) MPI module path.

ACTION
1) Confirm exact module environment and minimal repro.
2) Explain ABI/module version mismatch.
3) Provide compile workaround with explicit include path.
4) Confirm successful compile with user.
5) Track packaging improvement for future stack.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Michael,

Please try compiling with an explicit MPI Fortran module include path:

mpif90 -I /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/gfortran/11.1.0 -c -DMPI precision.F90

(Using `.../gfortran/10.2.0` is also a valid workaround.)

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Be explicit this is a compatibility workaround, not ideal long-term packaging.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Compilation / MPI Module Issue
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The issue appears to be related to the MPI module file compatibility with the compiler version. Similar issues have been reported in the past where the MPI module file was not generated by the same compiler version being used [1][2][3][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the compatibility of the MPI module file with the compiler version.
2. Check if there are any known issues with the specific MPI module and compiler combination.
3. If the issue persists, consider using `mpiifort` instead of `mpif90` as a quick fix [4].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm the exact versions of the MPI library and the compiler being used?
- Have you tried using `mpiifort` instead of `mpif90`?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Michael,

The issue you're experiencing is likely due to the MPI module file not being compatible with the compiler version you are using. This has been a known issue in the past [1][2][3][4].

Please try the following steps:
1. Verify the compatibility of the MPI module file with the compiler version.
2. Check if there are any known issues with the specific MPI module and compiler combination.
3. If the issue persists, consider using `mpiifort` instead of `mpif90` as a quick fix [4].

Please let us know if you need further assistance.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the MPI module file is compatible with the compiler version being used.
- Do not proceed with manual changes to the module file without verification.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-22110::chunk::0002
[2] : HPCSSUP-10400::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-82204::chunk::0000
[4] : HPCSSUP-11329::chunk::0002

[TICKET_SUMMARY] mpi_f08

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hello,
I am trying to compile a code that requires the mpi_f08 module for Fortran MPI support on the HPC (icelake).
I loaded the module openmpi/4.1.5/intel/b42idtrx, which is supposed to have this. However when I try to compile my code (which I have already compiled on different machines and everything works fine), I get the following error:
f951: Fatal Error: Reading module '/usr/local/software/spack/spack-rhel8-20210927/opt/spack/linux-rocky8-icelake/intel-2021.6.0/openmpi-4.1.5-b42idtrxnxnhjeplfsf7xjlghp5kwvkz/lib/mpi_f08.mod' at line 1 column 2: Unexpected EOF
compilation terminated.
I believe this occurs when the code asks to use mpi_f08.mod for the first time.
Do you know how I could solve this?
Thank you!
Valeria

[TICKET_SUMMARY] compilation

[CONTEXT]
4.4-intel-17.0.4-cesnirf netcdf-4.4.1.1-intel-17.0.4-zysrbqw panoply/4.5.1
[ahe24@login-e-10 SOURCE]$ cd ..
[ahe24@login-e-10 HYBRID]$ ./qrm: cannot remove ‘HYBRID9.exe’: No such file or directorympif90 -O3 -c INIT.f90INIT.f90(11): error #7013: This module file was not generated by any release of this compiler.   [MPI]

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0004 role=OTHER>
Jeffrey, do we have something wrong with our mpi wrappers here?
/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/intel/2017.4/compilers_and_libraries_2017.4.196/linux/mpi/intel64/bin/mpif90
is doing
ifort -I/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/intel/2017.4/compilers_and_libraries_2017.4.196/linux/mpi/intel64/include/gfortran/5.1.0  -I/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/intel/2017.4/compilers_and_libraries_2017.4.196/linux/mpi/intel64/include
which seems internally inconsistent (in the case of this code, removing the first "gfortran" -I allows the objects to build). Also I'm not sure how at an earlier point the user got it to build. I may be missing something obvious.
 
Thanks
Stuart

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0005 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Yes that does seem to be a problem with the wrapper. I think the install process may have gone wrong, it is meant to generate a version of the mpi module for each compiler. Did 2017.4 get installed with Kyle's special script?
A quick fix is to use mpiifort rather than mpif90.

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0006 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Annemarie,
Can you try using mpiifort rather than mpif90 as the fortran compiler? 
Best wishes,
Jeffrey.

</MESSAGE>

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Fortran MPI module problems

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
A user in TCM has complained that he cannot compile Fortran MPI code on 
the HPCS at the moment, and nor can I. He claims that he could 
yesterday.
There seems to be a module issue.
[mjr19@login-e-13 hello]$ pwd
/home/mjr19/hello
[mjr19@login-e-13 hello]$ mpif90 hello.mpi.f90 
hello.mpi.f90(3): error #7013: This module file was not generated by any 
release of this compiler.   [MPI]
use mpi
------^
compilation aborted for hello.mpi.f90 (code 1)
Using strace reveals that ifort 17.0.4 is using
/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/intel/2017.4/compilers_and_libraries_2017.4.196/linux/mpi/intel64/include/gfortran/5.1.0/mpi.mod
which was presumably created by/for gfortran 5.1.0. Mixing .mods between 
different Fortran compilers does not work.
We are both using the default set of loaded modules.
Regards,
Michael
– 
Dr MJ Rutter, TCM, Cavendish Lab, JJ Thomson Av, Cambridge. CB3 0HE
Email: mjr19@cam.ac.uk  Ph: 01223 337386  www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/~mjr

[ahe24@login-e-10 HYBRID]$ ./qrm: cannot remove ‘HYBRID9.exe’: No such file or directorympif90 -O3 -c INIT.f90INIT.f90(11): error #7013: This module file was not generated by any release of this compiler.   [MPI]

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0004 role=OTHER>
Jeffrey, do we have something wrong with our mpi wrappers here?
/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/intel/2017.4/compilers_and_libraries_2017.4.196/linux/mpi/intel64/bin/mpif90
is doing
ifort -I/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/intel/2017.4/compilers_and_libraries_2017.4.196/linux/mpi/intel64/include/gfortran/5.1.0  -I/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/intel/2017.4/compilers_and_libraries_2017.4.196/linux/mpi/intel64/include
which seems internally inconsistent (in the case of this code, removing the first "gfortran" -I allows the objects to build). Also I'm not sure how at an earlier point the user got it to build. I may be missing something obvious.
 
Thanks
Stuart

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0005 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Yes that does seem to be a problem with the wrapper. I think the install process may have gone wrong, it is meant to generate a version of the mpi module for each compiler. Did 2017.4 get installed with Kyle's special script?
A quick fix is to use mpiifort rather than mpif90.

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0006 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Annemarie,
Can you try using mpiifort rather than mpif90 as the fortran compiler? 
Best wishes,
Jeffrey.

</MESSAGE>

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Job Execution / MPI under Slurm
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance with documented launch pattern)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User attempted direct `mpirun` and saw MPI startup/initialisation errors. For this environment, MPI jobs should be run inside a Slurm allocation using the documented submission pattern. Running directly from a login shell is a common cause of PMPI/launcher errors.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Confirm launch context
   - Ask whether command was run:
     - directly on a login node, or
     - inside a Slurm job allocation.

2) Provide documented MPI job pattern
   - Instruct user to submit via `sbatch` with account/partition/resources set.
   - Recommend launching tasks with `srun` inside the job script.

3) Provide minimal working template
```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -A <ACCOUNT>
#SBATCH -p <PARTITION>
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --time=00:10:00

module purge
module load <required-modules>

srun ~/meme-mpi/bin/meme --help
```

4) If user needs interactive testing
   - First allocate resources interactively (documented method), then run with `srun` in that allocation.

5) Reference documentation
   - Share the MPI jobs section for the relevant partition.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Milena,

This error usually occurs when MPI is launched outside a proper Slurm allocation.

Please run your MPI command through a Slurm job script, for example:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -A <ACCOUNT>
#SBATCH -p <PARTITION>
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --time=00:10:00

module purge
module load <required-modules>

srun ~/meme-mpi/bin/meme --help
```

Submit with:

`sbatch <script_name>.sh`

Please also refer to:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/a100.html?highlight=mpirun#jobs-requiring-mpi

Using the documented Slurm launcher pattern should resolve this startup issue.

Regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Avoid ad-hoc launcher advice that bypasses Slurm resource allocation.
- Keep examples aligned with documented partition/account usage.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: MPI / Execution Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is encountering an error when attempting to run an MPI-enabled process. The error message indicates a fatal error in PMPI_Init_thread, which is a common issue reported in several tickets [1][2][3][5]. This issue has been observed on both Icelake and Cclake nodes.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the user's environment and module configuration.
2. Check if the user has loaded the correct MPI module.
3. Refer to previous tickets for similar issues and their resolutions [1][2][3][5].
4. If the issue persists, escalate to a higher-level support team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- What is the exact submission script being used?
- Have you tried running the code on different types of nodes (e.g., Icelake, Cclake)?
- Can you provide the output of `module list`?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Milena,

Thank you for your message. The error you are encountering is a known issue with MPI-enabled processes on our nodes. We have seen similar issues reported in the past [1][2][3][5].

To help us troubleshoot further, could you please provide the following information:
- The exact submission script you are using.
- Whether you have tried running the code on different types of nodes (e.g., Icelake, Cclake).
- The output of `module list`.

We will use this information to better understand the issue and provide a resolution.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user has loaded the correct MPI module before running their code.
- Refer to previous tickets for similar issues and their resolutions to avoid duplicating efforts.

[5] : HPCSSUP-51479::chunk::0000

MPIDI_OFI_mpi_init_hook(1602)....:
insert_addr_table_roots_only(451): OFI get address vector map failed
----------
Some of my colleagues (who use a different code) also can’t get their code to run on the Icelake nodes and so they use the cclake nodes instead. I haven’t tried the cclake nodes but I prefer to get my code working on the Icelake nodes. Would you please advise on what might have gone wrong? And please let me know if I can provide any further information that could be useful. Thank you very much.
Yue-Kin
-------------
Yue-Kin Tsang
Applied Mathematics, Newcastle University
[https://www.mas.ncl.ac.uk/Yue-Kin.Tsang](https://www.mas.ncl.ac.uk/Yue-Kin.Tsang)

Hello,

[TICKET_SUMMARY] mpi4py crashes 

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear Sir or Madam, 
I have loaded python with the command 
> module load python-3.9.6-gcc-5.4.0-sbr552h 
and installed mpi4py with  
> pip install mpi4py 
However, when I test mpi4py with  
> python -c 'from mpi4py import MPI; print("OK")' 
I get the error 
Abort(1090831) on node 0 (rank 0 in comm 0): Fatal error in PMPI_Init_thread: Other MPI error, error stack:
MPIR_Init_thread(178): 
MPID_Init(1427)......: 
MPIR_pmi_init(167)...: PMI2_Job_GetId returned 14 
I think this is because mpi4py doesn't work with the version of MPI installed, see [https://stackoverflow.com/questions/38985018/mpi4py-only-works-under-mpiexec](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/38985018/mpi4py-only-works-under-mpiexec). 
Could you please let me know which version of MPI works? I ran this on one of the icelake machines (login-q-2). 
Best regards 
Namu Kroupa

user_input:
Hi,
I have noticed that me and my colleague are unable to connect to the RCS. We both are connecting by SFTP, rcs.uis.cam.ac.uk on port 22 and both get the same unable to connect message.
We have also had a ticket from a user in our department explaining that she is also unable to connect where she was able to last week.
Is there a problem with the service at the moment? Or has anything changed that might be causing this?
Thanks
Kind Regards,
Shaun Cooke (he/him)
IT Service Manager
Department of Plant Sciences
University of Cambridge
Downing Street, Cambridge, CB2 3EA
Tel: 44 (0)1223 748962 | stc48@cam.ac.uk<[stc48@cam.ac.uk](mailto:stc48@cam.ac.uk)>
[https://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk<https://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk/](https://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk%3Chttps://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk/)>
This email (together with any files transmitted with it) is intended only for the use of the individual(s) to whom it is addressed. It may contain information which is confidential and/or legally privileged. If you have received this email in error, please notify the sender and delete the original message.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Access / RCS Connectivity / SSH-MFA
Technical Action Required: No (user onboarding guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports that multiple people are unable to connect to `rcs.uis.cam.ac.uk` over SFTP/SSH, raising the possibility of either a broader service problem or account-specific access changes. The correct first response is to confirm whether there is an active service incident and, if not, gather examples from affected users to determine whether the problem is shared or user-specific.

ACTION
1) Check whether there is an active service issue affecting RCS SSH/SFTP access.
2) Ask for example usernames, client/method used, and the exact error messages seen by affected users.
3) If the service is healthy, distinguish between users who cannot access RCS at all and users with account-specific denial errors.
4) For individual users, confirm SSH/storage MFA enrolment if relevant and ask them to raise their own tickets for account-specific checks.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Shaun,

We’re first checking whether there is any broader issue affecting RCS SSH/SFTP access.

If the service itself is healthy, we’ll need the affected usernames, the client or method being used, and the exact error messages so we can tell whether this is a shared problem or an account-specific one.

For users who can access the web portal but not SSH/SFTP services, we may also need to confirm that SSH/storage MFA has been set up separately.

If any individual user continues to have problems, please ask them to raise their own ticket so we can check their account details directly.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not troubleshoot third-party user accounts through one reporter without user-specific consent/context.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RCS Connectivity
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
Multiple users are reporting issues connecting to the RCS via SFTP, indicating a potential service-wide problem [1][2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Check the RCS service status and any ongoing maintenance or outages.
2. If no known issues are found, escalate to the RCS team for further investigation.
3. Monitor for additional reports of connectivity issues.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm if you are able to connect to other university services or servers?
- Have you tried connecting from a different network or location?
- Are there any specific error messages or codes displayed when attempting to connect?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Shaun,

Thank you for reporting this issue. We are aware of similar reports from other users and are currently investigating the matter [1][2][3]. We will update you as soon as we have more information.

In the meantime, could you please confirm if you are able to connect to other university services or servers? Additionally, have you tried connecting from a different network or location?

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that any service-wide issues are communicated promptly to all affected users.
- Follow standard escalation procedures for service outages or disruptions.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-29314::chunk::0000

[3] : HPCSSUP-91273::chunk::0000
